programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A retired American football star is murdered, but his widow jeopardises Alexx's investigation by seizing the body, determined to have it cryonically frozen before an autopsy can be performed. Robbery initially appears to be the motive for the crime - but as events unfold, another possible reason emerges. Meanwhile, Stetler pressures Calleigh and Jake to end their relationship

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Return of the American drama following the work of the Naval Criminal Investigative Service. Gibbs and McGee travel to Russia to escort a computer expert connected to classified intel. However, the mission is compromised and they are forced to go off the grid while being hunted by Russian mercenaries. Meanwhile, the team in DC frantically tries to locate the missing pair. Mark Harmon and Sean Murray star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Keelson is found to have had a huge file labelled 'Callen, G', but it is destroyed remotely before Callen can retrieve it. The team is assigned to monitor Iranian terrorists trying to get their hands on a huge hidden fortune that belonged to the former shah, and the case appears to have a concrete link to Callen when a woman in the group's ranks is identified as being his sister Amy. Starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. An 11-year-old boy is abducted by a hooded man in a shopping centre, leading to fears a copycat child killer is on the loose in Sun Hill. Mike Wilson becomes prime suspect when a shrine to convicted murderer Linda Johnson and her deceased partner in crime is found at his home, but as Neil grows closer to his informant in her cell he discovers the case is not nearly as straightforward as it first appeared

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Michael's one-night stand is rushed into AAU after overdosing on cocaine, and Ric realises pursuing his relationship with Annalese will only intensify his colleague's erratic behaviour. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fleur's devious and deranged behaviour reaches crisis point as a worried Abs turns up at Tess's house. The volatile youngster grabs a knife and barricades herself in the bedroom with Sam when Sarah and Nina arrive on the scene. Meanwhile, Woody contacts Susan, who says there is a chance Matt is not his son. Guppy tries to speak to his father and Josh is appalled by conditions at the hospital

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Heaton calls a press conference to tackle the growing media interest in Linda Johnson's involvement in the Clark abduction case, unwittingly attracting the attention of her victims' relatives as well. A ransom note mysteriously appears at the station demanding Linda drop off £10,000 in the park to secure Dean's release, but the ensuing sting operation threatens to end in tragedy

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: When Phil comes home from Scotland the welcome is not as warm as he'd like. Cindy has a frustrating day without David. Alan is in deep water, while Robbie talks to the press

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ted and Kathy have a heart to heart. Mark does some babysitting and Simon starts a new job. Emotional turmoil drives Cindy to desperate acts, while Roy plans his marriage to Pat

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Brad Connor is devastated to learn he may be HIV positive, and makes a drunken pass at Marsha Stubbs. Joy Wilton discovers the joys of mixing work with pleasure

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The Dalbys' long spell of misfortune comes to an end as James stumbles on the cause of the disease afflicting the family's herd. Mrs Hall advises James to get on with it vis-a-vis Helen and warns him that he risks losing her, with Richard Edmundson still vying for her affections. James finally pops the question, but shudders at the thought of having to ask Mr Alderson's permission

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: An ageing film star decides to sell some antiques, much to the delight of Tinker, who is a big fan - but her house is robbed just before the auction. Ian McShane stars

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Veronica takes over running Lionel's company, and rehires Sandra and Jacko, who finds himself having a run-in with the law. Elmo uses his newfound wealth to open a wine bar

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Grace Brothers decides to start an in-house magazine with Mr Humphries as the editor, but the new publication causes rifts among members of the staff. Vintage comedy, starring John Inman, Mollie Sugden and Wendy Richard

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Having witnessed his sister Edie fail several driving tests, Seymour gallantly volunteers to help teach her. However, because he is unable to control a vehicle himself, he needs a qualified motorist to ride along. Michael Aldridge and Thora Hird star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Seymour comes up with another madcap scheme to harness the power of nature using an old sail, a pair of roller skates and, in Compo, a very gullible 'test pilot'. Michael Aldridge, Bill Owen and Peter Sallis star

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: A youngster refuses to undergo a life-saving transplant operation, much to the dismay of his parents, who try to overrule him by taking the matter to court - even though he meets all the legal requirements to make his own decision. In a separate case, two drug addicts accused of killing a boy for his mobile phone are found not guilty of murder

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Brian is suspended after assaulting a high-ranking officer in the Met, suspecting him of covering up a death in custody years before. Meanwhile, an Argentinian pistol recovered from the Thames is linked to two unsolved murders which took place 16 years apart - and with one of them having occurred in Gibraltar, Sandra takes the team - minus Brian - on an Iberian adventure to uncover the truth. Alun Armstrong, Amanda Redman, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star in the crime drama

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki is called to perform a post mortem on the body of a woman found in a river, and the discovery that she had recently given birth causes the pathologist to worry her child may be abandoned somewhere. Meanwhile, Harry investigates the apparent suicide of a lieutenant at a nearby Army base, and suspicions soon arise that the two cases may be linked. Starring Emilia Fox and Tom Ward

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Heaton calls a press conference to tackle the growing media interest in Linda Johnson's involvement in the Clark abduction case, unwittingly attracting the attention of her victims' relatives as well. A ransom note mysteriously appears at the station demanding Linda drop off £10,000 in the park to secure Dean's release, but the ensuing sting operation threatens to end in tragedy

Medical drama series, starring Michael Spence and Anna-Louise Plowman

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A shaken Tess returns to work and Woody spends the day with Matt, but lands himself in hot water when he agrees to take the youngster for a spin in an ambulance. Ellen steps in after Harry's au pair quits, and immediately bonds with his children. Meanwhile, Bruno teases Abs about Nina's past and makes unethical money out of a patient's rare illness. Medical drama, starring Will Thorp

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: First of an eight-part story. Smithy helps the victim of a knife attack on the Jasmine Allen estate and renews acquaintance with Carly Samuels, a girl he had been helping, who witnessed the incident. It soon emerges that Carly is afraid of her violent boyfriend and keen to start a new life miles away from London. Meanwhile, Beth and Nate encounter a young carer who seeks relief from his responsibilities by setting fire to cars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant and Kathy have a confidential chat while Cindy's plans for the future take a dramatic turn. Meanwhile, Roy recognises someone from the past

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Cindy meets a hitman. Kathy thinks Grant may be an alcoholic. Kathy tells Frankie she's no longer needed at the cafe. Frankie and Alan end up kissing

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Jeremy is forced to face his fear of heights head-on, and Army widow Joy Wilton gets a second chance at happiness, while Michael and Marsha's marriage shows further signs of crumbling. Starring Ben Nealon, Rob Spendlove, Denise Welch and Meera Syal

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James addresses the misconceptions of an awkward farmer who prefers to treat his own animals, and Siegfried faces a dilemma as James and Helen's honeymoon looms. A chance encounter with old friend Stewie Brannon ruins Siegfried's chances of landing a plum job with the county horse racing board

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A schoolgirl asks Lovejoy to sell some priceless 18th-century erotic drawings on her behalf, but he soon realises there is more to the request than meets the eye. Starring Ian McShane and Caroline Blakiston

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Jacko comes to the aid of a damsel in distress, and ends up getting a date in return. Sandra has a blazing row with her boyfriend, that could be the end of their relationship

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The employees are insulted by a suggestion that they take a wage cut, and decide to hold a rooftop demonstration. Comedy, starring Mollie Sugden and John Inman

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: When Clegg receives news of an old friend dying in America, Compo and Seymour join him on a journey down memory lane, recalling time spent at school walking on gates and climbing trees - forgetting, of course, that it was many years ago. Poignant instalament of the long-running comedy, featuring the final appearance of Joe Gladwin as Wally Batty

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo speculates why blood rushes to the head when upside down but not to the feet while standing up, so Seymour sets up an experiment to demonstrate the way the body works. Comedy, starring Bill Owen and Michael Aldridge

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: A woman leaves her estate to her son Alexander, who disappeared in Italy during World War Two on condition he claims the inheritance by 2010. When a mysterious stranger turns up at the funeral to claim the inheritance, he is called an imposter by the rest of the family. The quest for the truth finds Dalziel and Pascoe uncovering dark family secrets, including murder, betrayal, mad dogs, gay bars, young love and a revealing birthmark. Feature-length drama, starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan, with Margaret Tyzack, Jamie Glover and Jack Hedley

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: As the Gibraltar locals reel from the murder of shipping agent Gordon Fletcher, Brian and Gerry - last seen being carried off in a shipping container - find themselves stranded on the side of a Spanish mountain, and now face the problem of how to get back to the rest of the team. As Sandra attempts to unravel secrets stretching back over three decades and link what has now become three murders, she still has doubts about the innocence of charming entrepreneur Harry Truman - but that doesn't stop her joining him for lunch on his luxury yacht. Conclusion of a two-part story, with regulars Amanda Redman, Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson joined by Vincent Regan and George Irving

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Harry is called to Budapest by Hungarian human rights lawyer Anna Sandor to perform a second post-mortem on the body of a drowned prostitute. He begins to help his lover investigate the suspicious death, but finds himself implicated in her murder when she is killed in her bed. The pathologist calls on Leo for help as he is forced to retreat underground while continuing to work on the case

programme.desc: First of an eight-part story. Meanwhile, Beth and Nate encounter a young carer who seeks relief from his responsibilities by setting fire to cars

programme.desc: Lexie is furious that Dorothy has taken over the island in the middle of the loch for her latest business venture - a tai chi retreat - and enlists Golly to sabotage the scheme. Molly struggles to put her break-up behind her, and Paul is roped into the housework. Dawn Steele stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: First of an eight-part story. Smithy helps the victim of a knife attack on the Jasmine Allen estate and renews acquaintance with Carly Samuels, a girl he had been helping, who witnessed the incident. It soon emerges that Carly is afraid of her violent boyfriend and keen to start a new life miles away from London. Meanwhile, Beth and Nate encounter a young carer who seeks relief from his responsibilities by setting fire to cars

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric resigns from the board, and tells Annalese to stay with Michael. However, when his declining health causes him to collapse during surgery, his life is placed in the hands of his love rival. Oliver's desire to please Greg leaves him overestimating his abilities, and Jac finally accepts Sacha as a friend after they share a difficult shift

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A gang of racists uses fireworks to attack an Asian family. One of the thugs is burned and admitted to hospital, where Guppy bravely stands up for himself when he becomes the target of racial abuse. Mr O'Brien demands to know why Josh hit his son, and Luke warns Woody not to let Susan take advantage of his generosity. Medical drama, guest starring Kika Mirylees, Shobu Kapoor and Jodi Albert

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of eight. Smithy battles in vain to save Carly's life following the shooting and struggles with his feelings back at the station when he is tasked as the family liaison officer. A mobile unit is set up on the estate to help with community problems in light of the gang violence, and Beth's decision to bring in Harry the dog proves a winner with Nate when he realises how popular he is with the girls. Cleveland Campbell guest stars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Cindy feels imprisoned in her marriage, Tiffany discovers that Grant really cares, Phil's drinking gets further out of hand and Kathy takes Ben to stay with Pat

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The pressure finally takes its toll on Cindy as she makes a momentous decision she could live to regret. Phil continues his drinking and Frankie causes more trouble for Alan.

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Paddy says farewell to the Fusiliers. Complications during a live firing exercise results in a secret coming up to the surface. Starring Jerome Flynn

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James and Helen are on their honeymoon and start the tuberculin testing, while Siegfried helps Tristan prepare for his final exams in Edinburgh

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The corpse of a recently married man is found hidden in an antique suit of armour - much to Lovejoy's dismay. Drama, starring Ian McShane and Dudley Sutton

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Jacko's interest in football is revived when he dates a player - but he soon discovers the game means far more to her than he does. Giles and Gloria throw a party at Elmo's bar

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Old Mr Grace recognises a photo of Mr Humphries' mother and begins to suspect the head of menswear may be his son and heir. John Inman stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo pulls a piece of antique silver from the canal, and Seymour sets about recovering any other items down there, with the intention of returning them to their rightful owners

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: An afternoon driving around in Wesley's car proves to be tough on the feet. Compo is suffering from hard skin and places his faith in a herbal jelly from a gypsy healer. Bill Owen stars

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A forgetful clock-mender contacts the sleuth when he becomes the target of a couple of small-time crooks. Armed with advice from DCI Adams, Hetty starts her investigation at a video store and soon discovers that there is more to the case than meets the eye

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Police drama, starring John Simm. Sam experiences visions that seem to suggest a return to the 21st century, but wakes to find himself still stuck in 1973. His mood lifts when he comes face to face with the younger version of a killer he arrested in 2006 and realises he has the chance to prevent a crime before it happens - but his unorthodox methods surprise colleagues at the station. With Philip Glenister and Marc Warren

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens the 16-year-old case of an infamous getaway driver convicted of murdering his wife when new evidence suggests it could have been a miscarriage of justice. But Gerry, who was on the original investigation, refuses to believe the prisoner is anything but guilty. Meanwhile, Brian is in a reflective mood, convinced his days at Ucos are numbered after his actions against former police commander Embleton. Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star as the veteran coppers, with Amanda Redman as their boss

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Harry and Nikki investigate a shooting at the Dutch embassy, where several people are killed and the ambassador's daughter Klara is wounded. Complications arise when orders are given to keep the identity of a dead bodyguard a secret, and an outside pathologist is appointed to explore the case. As Klara recovers in hospital, she gradually begins to remember who was responsible for the incident. Kate Ashfield (Collision), Lorcan Cranitch (Cracker) and Matthew McNulty (Lark Rise to Candleford) guest star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of eight. Smithy battles in vain to save Carly's life following the shooting and struggles with his feelings back at the station when he is tasked as the family liaison officer. A mobile unit is set up on the estate to help with community problems in light of the gang violence, and Beth's decision to bring in Harry the dog proves a winner with Nate when he realises how popular he is with the girls. In a world of icons such as the Kardashians, he is determined that he will be Wales' next star. Currently though, he works behind his mother's deli counter and is having to cope with the realisation that running a deli is a lot harder than it looks. Comedy set in a cafe

programme.title: Fflam

programme.desc: A divorcee finds a new lease of life when his daughter buys him a course of fitness sessions for his 65th birthday. However, his new hobby threatens to drive a wedge between him and his close-knit group of friends. Starring Les Dennis and Liz McClarnon, with Aaron Cobham, Louis Emerick and Michael Starke

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple with a budget of £600,000 search for a rural home in the Lancashire countryside, and later Ginny Buckley visits the Victorian seaside resort of Blackpool

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Birmingham mother and son Julie and Rich take to the kitchen - she to push the boundaries and discover new flavours, he to learn new ideas to impress his vegan girlfriend

programme.title: The Bidding Room

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: I Can't Say My Name: Stammering in the Spotlight
programme.desc: BBC producer Felicity Baker has a stammer that she has spent her whole life trying to hide. She struggles to say her own name. Now she is stepping out in front of the camera for the first time to talk about what it is like to live with the condition - and she soon finds she is not alone. She meets a rugby player who blames his stammer for his aggressive behaviour on the pitch, a rapper who found music stopped him stammering and the comedy star Michael Palin, whose father stammered but never ever spoke about it

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal maestro Dominic Chinea gets to work on a crumbling cast-iron garden gate, whose design includes the badge of the Royal Lancers, while horologist Steve fletcher and leather expert Suzie Fletcher join up to restore a wristwatch awarded for bravery in the First World War. Upholsterer Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on a rocking chair that has been at the heart of a family home for decades, and Geoff Harvey is given a 1920s penny arcade game

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Gordon welcomes two more pairs - partners Caroline and Andrew, and brothers Sam and Sassan. Will either duo have what it takes to beat the board and walk away with the jackpot of £100,000?

programme.title: Zara McDermott: Revenge Porn
programme.desc: Zara McDermott shot to fame on Love Island in 2018. But as soon as she left the villa, Zara was told that naked photos she had shared with an ex-lover had started circulating worldwide. The incident left Zara devastated, ashamed and looking for answers. Here she sets out to understand more about the impact of revenge porn on victims and questions whether anyone can safely share intimate images again

programme.title: Catch Her If You Can
programme.desc: Documentary following an attempt to expose a successful London businesswoman's double life as a con artist. A suspicious invoice tipped off a potential victim, and inspired her to track down other parties who had been fleeced in the past. Together, they came up with a plan to expose the woman's misdeeds

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph visits a detached house in Bourne in Lincolnshire, Martel Maxwell takes a look around a mid-terrace in Walsall in the West Midlands and Martin Roberts explores a former library in Troedyrhiw, south Wales. They then witness all three properties going under auction before talking to the buyers about their plans for their acquisitions

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement agents shut down a filthy takeaway infested with mice, and investigate a salmonella outbreak that struck down more than 50 guests at a wedding. Narrated by Harry Hepple

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Tim Weeks provide advice to two teams competing at Wadebridge, Cornwall, while host Anita Manning hops on a steam train at Bodmin Railway to find out more about the Beeching cuts to the rail network

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel fears for Anita, Zara is upset about Emma and Luca's newfound spark and Colin intends to declare his love to his sugar baby - but Karen thinks he is making a mistake

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: A gifted, but neglected, teenager's education is jeopardised when she and her mother are made homeless. Ashamed of her plight, she struggles to find help, all the while concerned that she may not be able to study for A levels after all. Written by Lisa McMullin, with Tillie Amartey and Mina Anwar

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin goes house-hunting in the south Wales countryside with an itinerant couple who have £600,000 to find a rural retreat close to their growing grandchildren. While in the area, Jonnie heads to the south coast of Monmouthshire, and tries his hand at catching a salmon using the rare art of lave net fishing, a traditional skill which stretches back 400 years

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Husband-and-wife duo Faraj and Chloe are paired with chefs Ellis Barrie and Akis Petretzikis, and soon put to work in their respective kitchens. It's a fast-and-furious cook with both Faraj and Chloe keen to come out victorious. But it's all very much in the balance when the 20 Minute cook is over and the 10 Minute Challenge ingredients are revealed. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against each other to buy four new items - a vintage Steiff teddy bear, a two-headed Georgian nutcracker, a very special bottle of Chateau Palmer wine, and a retro mechanical tin toy. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Green Party

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Karen goes on a date with mystery man 'Jordan' - unaware she is sitting down for a drink with Lucas - and is so excited she takes a sneaky picture of him to show her family. Jay and Billy argue over who will take Janet to her audition - only for an accident to leave the girl stranded. Isaac comes up with an idea to help take Bailey's mind off Mother's Day, and local matchmaker Kim encourages Iqra to pursue Mila, before turning her attention to Bobby's love life

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Contestants cook their Signature Dish, and after tasting all five plates, judges Gregg Wallace and John Torode award not only a place in the quarter-finals for two participants, but also a MasterChef apron. The remaining trio take part in the Favourite Ingredient Challenge. Gifted with their chosen favourite ingredient, as well as a larder, they are tasked with creating one exceptional dish that showcases it. Arbitrators in this edition include 2015 series champion Simon Wood and finalist Tony Rodd

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Under the eye of the acerbic chef, contestants Sam and Sassan, twin sisters Margaret and Ann and Paul and Stu answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board - the more bars they build up, the more they can win up to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Medical journalist and chronic insomniac Michael Mosley journeys into the world of sleep, finding out what it is and what happens to someone if they do not get enough. Michael seeks to discover whether his own insomnia runs in the family, sending off a blood sample for DNA analysis, and meets Dr Eleanor Scott in Leeds who is doing research into a possible link between Type 2 diabetes and lack sleep. He also looks at possible ways in which people can go about getting a better night's sleep, including a visit to a project in Denmark to find out if a night under the stars can reset his body clock

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A small flat in Tooting, south London, a huge former care home near a large park in Liverpool and a fire-damaged house in Silverdale in Shropshire are all sold at auction. Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin meet the new owners to hear their plans

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers discover an illegal puppy farm in Cannock, putting profit over care. Thirty dogs and two racoons are found kept in filthy conditions, some of them locked in old cars. There's no water, food or in some cases ventilation. Plus, a rogue kitchen trader swindling customers out of thousands of pounds

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Ochuko Ojiri lead two teams competing at Ardingly Antiques Fair in West Sussex, as they hunt for potentially profitable items to take to auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion monitors their progress and also looks at the art and history of Persian carpets

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: When hearing-impaired Rosie is told her deafness is getting worse, she is desperate to learn sign language. However, she faces opposition from husband Nathan. Written by Charlie Swinbourne, with Sophie Stone and Kelvin Fletcher

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to create a piece for the home of a buyer who has returned to her family roots in Shropshire following a health scare

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah heads to Yorkshire to help a couple of retired teachers with a £450,000 budget find a country home and finally return to their family roots. Along the way, Sonali meets a young Dales farmer who is using his dairy herd to produce Icelandic-inspired Skyr yoghurt

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Health professionals Hayley and Sunni compete. The pair met on social media over their shared love of a plant-based diet, so in an all-vegan show contest are keen to take the ingredients they cook with all the time and elevate them to new heights. Paired up with chef Romy Gill, Hayley is keen to rustle up some mouth-watering vegan treats in the Green Pepper kitchen. In the Red Tomato kitchen, Sunni is paired with chef Jeremy Pang, who brings his own flair and style to Sunni's usual ingredients. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including an Edwardian shoe closet, a bronze racehorse, a 17th-century writing desk and Concorde service medals. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain hurdling great Colin Jackson, former England striker Darren Bent, ex-WBA Super Middleweight champion boxer George Groves and Commonwealth Games gold medal-winning swimmer Aimee Willmott join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The second quarter-final sees four talented heat-winners return to vie for a place in knockout week by participating in the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by William Sitwell, one of the UK's toughest food critics - is to cook a dish inspired by a holiday. Gregg Wallace and John Torode preside as the contenders endeavour to take another step closer to being crowned MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bailey is triumphant as she takes part in the first round of the school science competition - but her joy doesn't last when she returns home to bad news. Lucas is interviewed for a job by Caleb, who isn't fooled by the ex-convict's attempts to pose under a different name - but insists he turn up on Monday or else

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Under the eye of the acerbic chef, contestants Paul and Stu and couple Sophie and Claud answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board - the more bars they build up, the more they can win up to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by comic actress Amy Poehler, star and director of the rebellious new comedy Moxie and Oscar-nominated Hollywood Brit Minnie Driver, who is promoting her new podcast Minnie Questions. Call the Midwife's Leonie Elliott also guests, alongside Hollywood star Jennifer Garner and singer-songwriter Nick Jonas. With music from singer-songwriter Tom Odell

programme.title: Fern Brady: Power & Chaos
programme.desc: In front of a packed crowd in her native Scotland, the rising star delivers an energetic stand-up performance tackling issues of sexuality, feminism, power, Brexit, Britishness, Scottishness and nationality, all with her usual caustic wit and electric stage craft

programme.title: Mum's List (2016)
programme.desc: Young couple Kate and Singe's lives are turned upside down when Kate is diagnosed with an incurable breast cancer. Over the course of her final few months, she creates a list - writing her thoughts and memories down to help the man she loved create the best life possible for their two sons after she is gone. Drama based on the book by St John Greene, starring Rafe Spall, Emilia Fox and Elaine Cassidy

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon and the team meet semi-retired couple Becky and David to help them convert their drab old yurt into an a colourful and inviting bijou B&B

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents the show from Wrexham, North Wales, with experts Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian, and heads to Chester Cathedral to learn how spiders inspired artists

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team help people with a little land or a beautiful garden generate extra income by creating their own unique B&Bs. They begin by heading to the picturesque Wye Valley to help George and Al transform their battered old family camper van

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and the team are in Herefordshire to meet new parents Cheryl and Bill and help get their chalet on a hill from flat-pack to Scandinavian chic

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright's daily dose of workout sessions. Today, fitness routines to get the heart pumping, joined by Strictly Come Dancing professional Janette Manrara

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize: 11-12 Year Olds
programme.desc: Topics include pure and impure substances, and studying in Spanish

programme.title: Bitesize: 12-13 Year Olds
programme.desc: Chemical reactions and how rates of pay are calculated

programme.title: Bitesize: 13-14 Year Olds
programme.desc: Topics include the structure of the Earth and calculating density

programme.title: 1066: A Year to Conquer England
programme.desc: After months trapped in a port in northern France, the winds finally change and Duke William leads his vast invasion fleet across the Channel. Catastrophically, King Harold is 300 miles away in the north, having defeated the Vikings. Harold rushes back south, gathers a new army, and heads to Hastings to take on this new invasion. The two armies will meet at Battle in Sussex on October 14, 1066, and the outcome will change the shape of England and Europe for ever. Dan Snow presents

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Robshaw family enter the 1920s, sampling their first taste of the racy reputation of the decade as they host a jazz-age party fuelled by a menu of 14 different cocktails. The period has something new for everyone, with sweet treats for Fred, spicy food for Brandon in a 1920s curry house and the kicks and tricks of the Charleston for Miranda and Ros with former Strictly Come Dancing stars James and Ola Jordan

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three semi-detached homes in York - including a two-up, two-down 1930s house reinvented for the modern market and a 1970s communal building - are in the spotlight

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham examines new scientific research that shows how subtle adaptations in the anatomy and physiology of big cats can contribute to the success of all stages of a hunt. He looks at how leg hairs help leopards stalk their prey, and how an enlarged area of lionesses' brains give them the edge

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Mark Stacey and Michael Baggott appraise an array of collectibles and antiques at Southwell Minster, Nottinghamshire, including a classic Rolling Stones LP and a haul of Russian silver. Presenter Paul Martin also finds time to explore the county's Newstead Abbey, once home to 19th-century poet Lord Byron

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mina Anwar, Glen Moore, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Shaun Williamson go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team transform an attic in the oldest house in the village of Wrington, North Somerset into a writer's retreat-themed B&B

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Northern Exposure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King travel on to the Baltic States, starting with Lithuania. They make a pilgrimage to the Hill of Crosses, a site historically under constant threat from the KGB. In Latvia the travelling cooks ride with the region's oldest biker gang, The Brothers of the Wind. Estonia sees the duo going back to basics on the island of Muhu. They get a taste of the future with an inventive chef, go foraging for sea kale and fall in love with a group of dancing grannies

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a bakery that produces 180,000 Cornish pasties a day, and discovers the very specific rule one of the pastries must comply with to genuinely be considered Cornish. Ruth Goodman debunks some myths about pasties and investigates the history of the pepper trade, while Cherry Healey learns about the science of onions - including why cutting them can bring tears to the eyes

programme.title: The Terror
programme.desc: Crozier demands that Franklin send out a sledge party to seek help, but he is reluctant to risk any more of his men. The resulting clash leads Franklin to condemn Crozier for his alcoholism, prompting his devastated old friend to take matters into his own hands, drafting a resignation letter and taking personal responsibility for finding help

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Comedy, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan. Mandy Carter is a woman with big dreams - and today her dream is to find work. But the only thing available to her is a job in a fruit packing factory, where a big mallet will come in handy. With Michelle Greenidge and Tom Basden

programme.title: Funny Festival Live
programme.desc: Stand-up performances by established and up-and-coming comedians, recorded in front of a virtual audience at a Covid-safe venue in south London. Tonight, Mancunian comedian Rachel Fairburn has a lesson in polite conversation, Darren Harriott discusses how to become a wine connoisseur, while Jo Brand explains the ins and outs of DIY enemas. Plus sketch group Britney make their TV debut

programme.title: Harry Hill's Lonely Island
programme.desc: Silent slapstick comedy short with Harry Hill appearing as a monkey in a tree trying to grab an apple

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: David Brent: Life on the Road (2016)
programme.desc: More than a decade on from his appearance in documentary series The Office, a film crew is once again following the life of David Brent. He is now working as a rep for a cleaning-goods distributor, but harbours dreams of making it big in the music business. Acting on this, he takes three weeks off work and tours with a new-look version of Foregone Conclusion, the band he fronted in the 1990s. Comedy, directed by and starring Ricky Gervais

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The six remaining designers are put into teams of two and challenged to transform three shops - a pet accessories shop, a camera store, and a kitchen equipment retailer. Each designer must make a pitch to the business owner they have been assigned to, meaning that three contenders will get their idea approved, while the others act as their assistants. Helping Michelle Ogundehin decide who should stay or go this week is guest judge and pop-up retail guru Ross Bailey

programme.title: Trump Takes On the World
programme.desc: After one of the most shocking presidencies in history, Donald Trump's top advisers and the leaders who clashed with him lift the lid on the critical moments of his foreign policy. In the third and final programme, Trump sets his sights on Asia, and determined to go ahead with his new tough approach, he launches the biggest trade war in economic history. Trump's unorthodox style is on full display as he switches between maximum pressure and maximum flattery, as he forms the most unlikely of friendships with one of the world's most brutal dictators, Kim Jong-un

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate the case of a man who thought he had found love on a dating site with a woman who began making demands for money

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts the semi-final of the cooking challenge. Mary Berry challenges the remaining contestants to prepare a fish supper, Chris Bavin asks them to come up with a meal that puts mushrooms front and centre, and Angela Hartnett sets them the task of making crab ravioli with homemade pasta dough. After a dramatic end to the final challenge, one more celebrity leaves the kitchen

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a young canine-loving couple from Hertfordshire, who are hoping for a fresh start in a new High Weald home, and have a budget of £600,000

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents the final of the contestant, which sees the three remaining contestants aiming to become top dog-groomer - and win themselves the Golden Stanley trophy to prove it. All they have to do is execute one of the toughest challenges in the industry - a continental clip on an elegant standard poodle. As if that were not enough, they have to guess a breed of dog while blindfolded, before one last dogwalk parade. Who will impress judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor the most?

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright's daily dose of workout sessions continues with his demonstration of how to improve flexibility and balance

programme.title: Politics Live

programme.desc: Big Read with Dina Asher-Smith, Shakespeare's use of language and design and technology

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The family discover a surprising decade of progress and optimism as they journey through the 1930s, with their larder now stuffed with familiar brands and snacks. Brandon and Rochelle take a trip out for dinner in their very first family car and there is popcorn to go with their own home cinema, but the family's old servant Debbie finds out that the 1930s bring hard times for working women such as her. However, as 1939 comes around the family's hopes for the future are dashed as their final party is interrupted by an historic radio announcement

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Robshaws enter the 1940s and experience a decade dominated by war and entirely fuelled by rations, including powdered egg, nettles on toast and imitation brawn. They are even one family member down as Fred is evacuated to the countryside, but the war brings them together too as they share a Woolton pie in their very own Anderson shelter, the girls go dancing with Debbie and some GIs, and they all start to recognise how the family meal to bring them together in the good and bad times

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties are thrown into the mix in York - an eco-friendly futuristic dream home, a converted barn with a modern twist and a five-storey Georgian townhouse

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores the ingenious ways in which ants have collaborated to colonise the planet like no other animal. Natural air conditioning systems keep them cool in their nests, shelters made from their own bodies protect them from the elements, and a strong sense of smell helps them to overpower animals hundreds of times larger than themselves. Plus, a look at recent research that reveals how ant colonies are capable of immunising themselves against certain diseases

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents the show from Crowcombe Court, west Somerset, where experts James Lewis and Thomas Plant discover some promotional Victorian sausage plates

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mina Anwar, Glen Moore, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Shaun Williamson test their general knowledge skills on their penultimate day of head-scratchers

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon and his team travel to rural Oxfordshire to meet Asiyla and her family and help transform an old sailing boat into unique glamping accommodation.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Northern Exposure
programme.desc: As the travellers head for Russia, they swap vehicles for Dave's old motorbike and sidecar before spending a night backstage at the ballet, courtesy of one of Russia's most powerful oligarchs. Si and Dave also take a walking history lesson courtesy of St Petersburg's biggest fashion designers, and taste some of the country's finest foods

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Mandy and Chris adore their Victorian home in Manchester, but they cannot unlock the potential of their garden. Overlooked on all sides, the couple feel exposed whenever they spend time in it. Providing inspiration for the area are designers Helen Elks-Smith and Pip Probert. Meanwhile, Joel Bird meets Ben and Elaine, a Wiltshire couple who have an unusual outdoor space. Angela Scanlon hosts

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti work at London's Shangri-La Hotel, Britain's highest hostelry. Their expectations of a standard business guest experience are blown away by discovering a hotel that makes guests' personal moments special. From bath butlers to marriage proposals, the place is a resort for celebrations in the clouds

programme.title: Funny Festival Live
programme.desc: Nabil Abdulrashid is at the helm with three comics on stage at a Covid-safe Comedy Club. Tim Renkow presents the rough guide to wheelchair use on public transport. Catherine Bohart dissects the pan sexual scale. Zoe Lyons has acquired some new skills, and Nabil rounds things off with an insight into what it is to be a 'practicing' black man. Filmed at the Bedford pub in Balham, South London

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emma Barnett

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys D'Movie (2014)
programme.desc: The indomitable Irish matriarch discovers that a plan is afoot by developers to buy the famous Dublin street market where her family has run a fruit and veg stall for generations. Assembling a band of eccentric allies, she plans to fight back, only to realise her daughter is not interested in taking over the family business. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts as the remaining three contestants take part in the final as they compete to be crowned the first-ever winner of the competition, with the cooks having to impress Mary Berry, Angela Hartnett and Chris Bavin if they're to win the title. First, the competitors take on the Rustle Up challenge, creating dishes that showcase butternut squash as the main ingredient, before being asked to produce their ultimate picnic and presenting their creations to the judges at a beautifully decorated picnic tables. Who will be crowned the winner?

programme.title: Being Bridget Jones
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating 25 years of Helen Fielding's hapless creation. The programme examines the ways in which society's attitudes to women have shifted in 25 years, along with the ways in which female writers have told their own stories since Bridget made her debut. Featuring interviews with Helen Fielding, and the friends she based the original characters on, as well as tributes from celebrity fans

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon and his team travel to rural Oxfordshire to meet Asiyla and her family and help transform an old sailing boat into unique glamping accommodation.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah showcases a selection of Cotswolds properties to a couple from Essex who are hoping their budget of £550,000 will buy them a new home with three bedrooms, one of which could be used for a home office. The presenter also visits a quarry from which Cotswold stone is used for new builds and conservation projects

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Windermere Jetty Museum in the Lake District, where items assessed include a collection of silver trophies awarded to a pioneering female sailor. There is also a James Bond poster scraped off a wall, a guitar rescued from a skip, a wooden dingy from the film Swallows & Amazons, and a pair of necklaces with surprising values

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: For today's exercises, Mark Wright is joined by Strictly and reality TV star Jamie Laing

programme.title: Bitesize: 11-12 Year Olds
programme.desc: Energy stores in physics, guiding the reader in English, and finding inspiration within ourselves in music

programme.title: Bitesize: 12-13 Year Olds
programme.desc: The science of sound waves and leitmotifs in music

programme.title: Bitesize: 13-14 Year Olds
programme.desc: Magnetic fields in science and writing song lyrics in music

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The family reflect on their time travels, discovering that much of what they experienced in the past, still lives on around our dinner tables today. Polly Russell shows them the delights of a contemporary canned meal, and Brandon and Rochelle sample a Peruvian dinner with co-presenter Giles Coren. Finally the Robshaws share a celebratory meal with Giles and Polly as they realise how many of our historical eating habits are still a treasured part of modern family mealtimes

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Chuko Ojiri hopes a wooden beetle will fly at the auction, while a job lot of broken crockery threatens disaster for Eric Knowles in the week's finale

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Eric Knowles takes on Danny Sebastian at an antiques fair in Nottinghamshire, with the two taking very different approaches to buying and selling

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: In York, the winners of the best detached, best apartment, best semi-detached and best wildcard property are revisited by the judges for a second inspection. The arbiters must make their ultimate decision and crown only one of them the week's Best House in Town

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Claire Rawle and Thomas Plant look out for potential treasures at Chiddingstone Castle in Kent, while presenter Paul Martin visits a hop garden and hears about the thousands of Londoners who descended on farms to pick the hops in times gone by

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mina Anwar, Glenn Moore, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Shaun Williamson keep their eyes on the prize as Richard Osman reveals who is the week's winner

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: In the Cotswolds, Simon Parfett and his team meet Tammy and Colin. Wanting to spend more time together as a family and create extra income, they decided to buy a horsebox which they could rent out to festival-goers. Alas, things didn't go to plan, so Simon and company come to the rescue

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Reece Parkinson and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Broadcaster and rambler Stuart Maconie walks along the River Hodder in the Forest of Bowland, enjoying the scenery and examining intriguing myths and snippets of history

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don and the team look back at more floral highlights from 2020, including alliums and auriculas, houseplants and creative containers, as well as the scented stars of the summer garden. There's also another chance to see what the Gardeners' World viewers were getting up to in their gardens last year as they share their horticultural adventures

programme.title: The Comedy Vaults: BBC2's Hidden Treasure
programme.desc: As part of the channel's 50th birthday celebrations, this programme delves into the archives for rarely seen moments from some of the greatest names in comedy. Featuring clips from unbroadcast pilots including Miranda Hart's Joke Shop, Stephen Fry's first appearance as QI host and a sitcom starring the band Madness, as well as footage of Spike Milligan, Peter Cook and Dudley Moore, Rik Mayall, Billy Connolly, and Sacha Baron Cohen. Tamsin Greig narrates

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: The host chats to award-winning British songstress Laura Mvula, who discusses her favourite performances from the Later archive. Plus, South London four-piece Dry Cleaning make their TV debut. And singer/songwriter Paul Weller performs a track from his new LP, Fat Pop (Volume 1) at his Black Barn studios in Surrey

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Festival of Funny: Shorts
programme.desc: A bold, contemporary collection of the silliest, most distinctive comedy short films. Platforming the next generation of comedy voices, the films showcase the most exciting creative talent from all across the UK and beyond

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Tyrol seeks advice from the imprisoned Baltar after clashing with Admiral Adama over the need for safer working conditions on board the ship, and Seelix becomes involved in the argument after being turned down for flight training

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Starbuck's mental stability causes concern with Admiral Adama and Lee as she struggles to come to terms with her time spent in captivity on New Caprica

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team help people with a little land or a beautiful garden generate extra income by creating their own unique B&Bs

programme.title: Miss World 1970: Beauty Queens and Bedlam
programme.desc: The story of the protest at the contest, when protestors dramatically disrupted the event and kickstarted a feminist revolution in front of an audience of millions. Hannah Berryman's film uses archive, animation and contributions from key players to offer an entertaining and thought-provoking celebration of a night of game-changing anarchy

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: The steeplejack shares his appreciation for the skills of the men who built Britain's great castles, palaces and country houses. He draws on his experiences as an apprentice joiner to offer demonstrations of building techniques, including the humorous falling-down arch

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross's exciting use of blue, yellow and orange hues brings vibrancy to a cabin in a woodside setting beside a clear blue lake

programme.title: Armada - 12 Days to Save England
programme.desc: Dan Snow relives the events of the naval battle in 1588, drawing on recently rediscovered documents to get a sense of the key events in the conflict and exposing the tensions among the Spanish commanders. Dramatic reconstructions go behind the scenes in Elizabeth I's royal court, revealing the impact the prospect of invasion had on the queen and her advisors as she tried to maintain a strong public image while under the threat of an assassination attempt. With analysis from Professor Saul David and Dr Sam Willis

programme.title: Elizabeth R
programme.desc: Award-winning drama chronicling the reign of Elizabeth I. Mary Queen of Scots is imprisoned at Chartley, while Elizabeth ponders her fate. Starring Glenda Jackson, Vivian Pickles, David Collings and Stephen Murray

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth's Secret Agents
programme.desc: Documentary featuring dramatic reconstructions and interviews with historians to examine the network of spymasters and secret agents that helped protect Elizabeth I from assassination, terror and treason for more than 40 years. The first edition reveals how the pope's declaration of Elizabeth as a heretic 12 years into her reign set England's Catholics against her, leading the queen to charge William Cecil with the task of stopping assassins getting through, and the huge espionage network he established saw his spies break Catholic conspiracies at home and abroad

programme.title: The Story of Welsh Art
programme.desc: Huw Stephens explores how, from the 18th century, the dramatic landscape of Wales captivated artists like Richard Wilson and JMW Turner, who painted it in ways that changed the course of art history. Huw also discovers the work of Penry Williams, a Merthyr Tydfil artist who was commissioned to paint his home town's vast Cyfarthfa ironworks

programme.title: Women in Film: BBC Introducing Arts
programme.desc: Cultural historian Janina Ramirez presents a collection of intriguing and exciting short films by emerging women directors and artists. Each film gives a female perspective on modern day topics from body image and new love to grief and belonging

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross's exciting use of blue, yellow and orange hues brings vibrancy to a cabin in a woodside setting beside a clear blue lake

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: The steeplejack shares his appreciation for the skills of the men who built Britain's great castles, palaces and country houses. He draws on his experiences as an apprentice joiner to offer demonstrations of building techniques, including the humorous falling-down arch

programme.title: Armada - 12 Days to Save England
programme.desc: Dan Snow relives the events of the naval battle in 1588, drawing on recently rediscovered documents to get a sense of the key events in the conflict and exposing the tensions among the Spanish commanders. Dramatic reconstructions go behind the scenes in Elizabeth I's royal court, revealing the impact the prospect of invasion had on the queen and her advisors as she tried to maintain a strong public image while under the threat of an assassination attempt. With analysis from Professor Saul David and Dr Sam Willis

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Mark Horton travels to Gwynedd, where he examines the history of Harlech Castle, revealing what it would have looked like during the 13th century when it was used to subdue the Welsh, while Neil Oliver visits its nearby counterpart Criccieth

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: An insight into the steeplejack's appreciation for the craftsmanship and perfectionism of the past and for the work of those people who still carry on the traditions today

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American painter Bob Ross ensures that a playful brook becomes the perfect complement for a range of rugged mountains

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James manages to arrange a date with Helen after she visits the surgery with her dog Dan, while Tristan runs into problems with an awkward customer and Siegfried's temper fails to improve when he tries to give up smoking

programme.title: Screen One
programme.desc: Hancock, by William Humble. Drama detailing the last eight years of comedy actor Tony Hancock's life, encompassing the airing of the most famous of his Hancock's Half-Hour episodes, The Blood Donor, and leading to his untimely death in June 1968. It is argued that his refusal to accept the limitations of his own genius destroyed his two marriages and led to the 44-year-old's suicide in a Sydney flat. With Alfred Molina and Frances Barber

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To
programme.desc: Interview with comedy writers Ray Galton and Alan Simpson, who created memorable shows including Steptoe and Son and Hancock's Half Hour. The pair discuss their split and recall their response when they discovered Tony Hancock had committed suicide

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Treasures
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank's journey takes him to India and Sri Lanka, where he examines the cultural importance of sex and fertility in Hindu countries. After encountering a statue of a 10-armed naked goddess in the Cave of Heavenly Maidens he discovers the secrets of the universe in Jaipur and revels in a palace of smells. His tour ends at one of the greatest monuments to love ever created - the Taj Mahal

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American painter Bob Ross ensures that a playful brook becomes the perfect complement for a range of rugged mountains

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: An insight into the steeplejack's appreciation for the craftsmanship and perfectionism of the past and for the work of those people who still carry on the traditions today

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: An edition first shown in July 2012 focusing on Curiosity, Nasa's mission to investigate the possibility of life on Mars. The agency hoped to land a nuclear-powered rover vehicle, which cost $2.5billion to develop, on the surface of the planet by winching it down from a rocket-powered crane, then use it to explore the terrain. The programme features contributions by the project's chief scientist John Grotzinger and discovers the lessons experts learned from previous expeditions, many of which ended in failure

programme.title: World of Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver reports on a 5,000-year-old temple discovered in Orkney. Built 500 years before Stonehenge, the undisturbed artefacts found there, including wall decorations, pigments and paint pots, are helping to increase knowledge and understanding of the Neolithic people. Special effects are used to re-create the structure in 3D, allowing Neil to walk inside and explore it

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: A look at the steeplejack's admiration for ordinary workers. From the labourers and stonemasons who built great medieval castles, to 20th-century coal miners and mill workers, Fred recognises that without hard graft nothing would have been possible

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: A celebration of the later part of the decade's psychedelic music, featuring counter-culture classics by the Who, Pink Floyd, Joe Cocker, the Nice and the Jimi Hendrix Experience

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first aired on October 4, with performances by artists including Twenty 4 Seven featuring Captain Hollywood, Pet Shop Boys, MC Tunes versus 808 State, Hi Tek 3 featuring Ya Kid K, MC Hammer, Technotronic, Bobby Vinton and Maria McKee

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first aired on October 11, with performances by artists including the Sisters of Mercy, the Chimes, the Beautiful South, Neneh Cherry, Monie Love featuring True Image, Cliff Richard, Status Quo, New Kids on the Block, Maria McKee and Depeche Mode

programme.title: Meat Loaf: In and Out of Hell
programme.desc: A profile of the rock star's life and career, reflecting on the music that propelled him to fame and charting his journey from an overweight, bullied child to his larger-than-life persona and icon status. Featuring an interview and performance by the singer, as well as backstage footage from a Las Vegas concert. The film also revisits the Dallas of Meat Loaf's early years and includes insights from high school friends who reveal where the unusual moniker really came from

programme.title: Eric Burdon: Rock 'n' Roll - Animal
programme.desc: How pop group the Animals were important standard bearers of the 'British Invasion' of America, behind the Beatles but ahead of the Rolling Stones, the Who and the Kinks. Alongside his passion for original American blues, frontman Eric Burdon got together in the late 1960s with the black LA band WAR - itself a political statement in the Black Panther era - and, inspired by Charlie Parker, John Coltrane and Rahsaan Roland Kirk, expanded his musical spectrum with jazz and funk. This documentary reveals how Burdon's creative output has made an important and profoundly authentic contribution to popular culture

programme.title: Robert Plant: By Myself
programme.desc: The singer discusses his varied life and musical career, including his upbringing in the West Midlands town of Stourbridge, his rise to stardom as the singer of influential rock band Led Zeppelin and his solo projects. The programme also reflects on his collaboration with country singer Alison Krauss, with whom he made the Grammy-winning album Raising Sand, and his reunion with virtuoso guitarist Jimmy Page

programme.title: Southern Rock at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances by classic Southern rock acts on shows including The Old Grey Whistle Test, In Concert and Wogan. Featuring Lynyrd Skynyrd, the Charlie Daniels Band, the Ozark Mountain Daredevils, Delaney & Bonnie with Eric Clapton, Dickey Betts, formerly of the Allman Brothers Band, the Marshall Tucker Band, Black Oak Arkansas, Greg Allman with Cher, and Edgar Winter

programme.title: Meat Loaf: In and Out of Hell
programme.desc: A profile of the rock star's life and career, reflecting on the music that propelled him to fame and charting his journey from an overweight, bullied child to his larger-than-life persona and icon status. Featuring an interview and performance by the singer, as well as backstage footage from a Las Vegas concert. The film also revisits the Dallas of Meat Loaf's early years and includes insights from high school friends who reveal where the unusual moniker really came from

programme.desc: Children's animation Gluko and Lennon, two inseparable buddies who get up to all sorts of crazy adventures in their home of Townopolis

programme.title: Danger Mouse

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Dennis can’t get Grizzly’s new animal training method to work on wilful Gnasher. However, when they realise that Gnasher can use the method to get humans to obey him instead, they exploit this new power to the max.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: The kids' mean neighbor, Wanda, has started enforcing a dogs-on-leashes law. Boy, Girl, Cheese and Mouse each come up with ideas about how to show that Dog is special and doesn’t need a leash.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: Specialist agents investigate all sorts of strange and weird happenings in their town by calling on their skills in mathematics and numeracy

programme.title: Danny & Mick
programme.desc: Danny and Mick try to raise money for a good cause, but succeed in raising laughs, eyebrows and hair instead

Dr Chris finds out how scientists are making a new vaccine to help the Covid-19 pandemic, and there is a broken wrist and dislocated shoulder in the emergency department.

programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.desc: Good news - it's possible to levitate! Bad news - there are some serious side effects

programme.title: Celebrity Supply Teacher
programme.desc: Jockey Khadijah Mellah delivers a masterclass on horses and horse riding. Khadijah won the Magnolia Cup at only 19 years old, and she describes what that moment was like and the training that was required to get there. She gives expert tips on how to approach and groom a horse and demonstrates the finer points of basic riding.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: The life of the English tribal queen, following her as she turns against her Roman masters and unleashes her forces on Colchester, St Albans and London. Plus, Roman emperors Caligula and Nero dish out their own unique brand of imperial nastiness. Starring Lorna Watson

programme.title: Our School
programme.desc: Following youngsters as they make the transition from primary to secondary school and begin the most important year of their school lives so far

programme.title: Ricky Wilson's Art Jam
programme.desc: TV presenter and baker extraordinaire Liam Charles visits the Art Jam studio to show Ricky three items that mean the world to him - his Great British Bake Off apron, his special baker’s palette knife and a 3D chocolate treat. While chatting to Ricky, Liam shares amazing stories about his time on Bake Off, his love of Harry Potter, his baking style and the celebrities he has made cakes for.

programme.title: Out of This World
programme.desc: In this episode, Dr Maggie takes us into space to study Earth and see how it has helped us to understand climate change. Dr Maggie’s star guest is Susanne Mecklenburg, who is in charge of analysing Earth’s climate at the European Space Agency. All your space questions are answered in Ask Maggie, and she counts down the top five surprising space facts.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Dr Chris dresses up as a giant tongue in the lab and our Ouch Babies celebrate their first birthdays! We meet the first of our amazing new Ouch Patients and show you what to do if someone falls in first aid. And find out what happens in the emergency department after Lucy got knocked over by a car.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse's attempts at gardening lead to a gigantic Welsh space plant taking over the world and an attack of intergalactic bees. Only Penfold's jam obsession can save the day

programme.title: The Deep
programme.desc: While fighting over Viking treasure, the Nektons take over the Dark Orca. The pirates in turn take over the Aronnax. Neither side is happy about strangers in their homes, but neither is willing to back down

programme.title: The Deep
programme.desc: The Nektons explore a floating island and find the final piece of the Ephemychron in a cave. However, the question remains of why the island is moving

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: There's a pong in the lab as the Doctors reveal the special part of your brain that helps you smell. And we meet snowboarding Brilliant Body Aimee Fuller.

programme.title: We Bare Bears
programme.desc: After finding a bedazzled jean jacket in a dumpster, the bears fight over who gets to wear the 'lucky' garment

programme.title: Summer Camp Island
programme.desc: While attempting a science experiment, Hedgehog and Max get shrunk down and go to a high school for food where they must help photo journalist Cinnamon Raisin Toast go to prom with jock Popular Banana Split. Meanwhile, Oscar tries looking for his friends and ends up going feral

programme.desc: Today we meet a new family. Brother and sister, Noah and Isla, are playing at being train drivers with their cuddly toys as the passengers. The pretend train is off to London where the toys get to meet Queen Isla

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Cook and Line want to be more like Captain Captain, so things turn to chaos when they decide to dress-up and behave exactly like her

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels are playing with their toys when they notice that there are sheep in the clubhouse. Animation for children aged two to five

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: The bunny sets his sights on Mr McGregor's pumpkin patch, and rolling it out of the garden turns into an exciting ride

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bob is stuck in a runaway hot air balloon and needs clear instructions to land it

programme.title: What's On Your Head?
programme.desc: Lucy the helicopter paramedic shows us her helmet. She tells us all about how important her helmet is, and how it helps her to do her job and help people.

programme.title: Ferne & Rory's Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Xiangfeng wants to know all about amazing ants! Ferne visits a leaf cutter ant colony at the zoo, while Rory and his son Sandy head into the forest, looking for wood ants. Plus, we find out how ants play an important role in the growing of plants and crops. They are amazing soil engineers!

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. Mr Hammond has been taking photos with his new camera for an underwater sea-creatures exhibit happening at Safari World. Jen has been downloading them in the Aqua Base. When Andy and Jen are looking through them, they suddenly notice that instead of a picture of a weedy sea dragon hatching, there is a picture of Mr Hammond grinning awkwardly at the camera. He’s inadvertently taken a picture of himself instead of the weedy sea dragon. Just then, Mr Hammond calls to see how the downloading of the photos is going and specifically asks if they have seen his picture of the weedy sea dragon. A famous photographer is coming to open the exhibit and Mr Hammond wants to show her his photo of a baby sea dragon. Realising Mr Hammond’s mistake, Jen informs him that they are

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch and his grimbots have a long race along the Great Wall of China

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Dina Lady and The Lost Pirate join team captains Gail Force and Keith Fitt for fun clips and questions.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle ends up in Evie's class, when he must be still so the headteacher doesn't see him.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: When JoJo discovers that astronauts have visited the moon she decides she would like to go there too. With Gran Gran's help, she builds a homemade rocket and blasts off for an imaginary adventure into space.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The Bubble Man arrives in town, so a visit to the Big Shop is in order for Sarah, Duck and Donkey. The adventures of a seven-year-old girl and her duck

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Colly Wobble wants to try some different shoes and chooses roller skates. In Storyland Moon, Baby finds a magical shoe tree, with different shoes growing on it

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Major invents a machine that can send bubbles containing voice messages out into space. Animated fun, voiced by Michael Palin

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Igglepiggle goes for a long journey and gets lost. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Actress Ashley Jensen reads Paper Dolls, by Julia Donaldson and Rebecca Cobb

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple with a budget of £600,000 search for a rural home in the Lancashire countryside, and later Ginny Buckley visits the Victorian seaside resort of Blackpool

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Birmingham mother and son Julie and Rich take to the kitchen - she to push the boundaries and discover new flavours, he to learn new ideas to impress his vegan girlfriend
