programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Jez and Ieuz's trip leads them to Naples, the spiritual home of pizza. It's a matter of days before the competition, so they need to focus on tracking down the best ingredients for their pizza. They're also keen to pick up any last minute tips, so they arrange a series of masterclasses with some of the most renowned pizzaiolos in the city

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal maestro Dominic Chinea gets to work on a crumbling cast-iron garden gate, whose design includes the badge of the Royal Lancers, while horologist Steve fletcher and leather expert Suzie Fletcher join up to restore a wristwatch awarded for bravery in the First World War. Upholsterer Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on a rocking chair that has been at the heart of a family home for decades, and Geoff Harvey is given a 1920s penny arcade game

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Gordon welcomes two more pairs - partners Caroline and Andrew, and brothers Sam and Sassan. Will either duo have what it takes to beat the board and walk away with the jackpot of £100,000?

programme.title: I Can't Say My Name: Stammering in the Spotlight
programme.desc: BBC producer Felicity Baker has a stammer that she has spent her whole life trying to hide. She struggles to say her own name. Now she is stepping out in front of the camera for the first time to talk about what it is like to live with the condition - and she soon finds she is not alone. She meets a rugby player who blames his stammer for his aggressive behaviour on the pitch, a rapper who found music stopped him stammering and the comedy star Michael Palin, whose father stammered but never ever spoke about it

programme.title: Zara McDermott: Revenge Porn
programme.desc: Zara McDermott shot to fame on Love Island in 2018. But as soon as she left the villa, Zara was told that naked photos she had shared with an ex-lover had started circulating worldwide. The incident left Zara devastated, ashamed and looking for answers. Here she sets out to understand more about the impact of revenge porn on victims and questions whether anyone can safely share intimate images again

programme.title: Catch Her If You Can
programme.desc: Documentary following an attempt to expose a successful London businesswoman's double life as a con artist. A suspicious invoice tipped off a potential victim, and inspired her to track down other parties who had been fleeced in the past. Together, they came up with a plan to expose the woman's misdeeds

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph visits a detached house in Bourne in Lincolnshire, Martel Maxwell takes a look around a mid-terrace in Walsall in the West Midlands and Martin Roberts explores a former library in Troedyrhiw, south Wales. They then witness all three properties going under auction before talking to the buyers about their plans for their acquisitions

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement agents shut down a filthy takeaway infested with mice, and investigate a salmonella outbreak that struck down more than 50 guests at a wedding. Narrated by Harry Hepple

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Tim Weeks provide advice to two teams competing at Wadebridge, Cornwall, while host Anita Manning hops on a steam train at Bodmin Railway to find out more about the Beeching cuts to the rail network

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel fears for Anita, Zara is upset about Emma and Luca's newfound spark and Colin intends to declare his love to his sugar baby - but Karen thinks he is making a mistake

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: A gifted, but neglected, teenager's education is jeopardised when she and her mother are made homeless. Ashamed of her plight, she struggles to find help, all the while concerned that she may not be able to study for A levels after all. Written by Lisa McMullin, with Tillie Amartey and Mina Anwar

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin goes house-hunting in the south Wales countryside with an itinerant couple who have £600,000 to find a rural retreat close to their growing grandchildren. While in the area, Jonnie heads to the south coast of Monmouthshire, and tries his hand at catching a salmon using the rare art of lave net fishing, a traditional skill which stretches back 400 years

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Husband-and-wife duo Faraj and Chloe are paired with chefs Ellis Barrie and Akis Petretzikis, and soon put to work in their respective kitchens. It's a fast-and-furious cook with both Faraj and Chloe keen to come out victorious. But it's all very much in the balance when the 20 Minute cook is over and the 10 Minute Challenge ingredients are revealed. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against each other to buy four new items - a vintage Steiff teddy bear, a two-headed Georgian nutcracker, a very special bottle of Chateau Palmer wine, and a retro mechanical tin toy. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By Plaid Cymru

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Karen goes on a date with mystery man 'Jordan' - unaware she is sitting down for a drink with Lucas - and is so excited she takes a sneaky picture of him to show her family. Jay and Billy argue over who will take Janet to her audition - only for an accident to leave the girl stranded. Isaac comes up with an idea to help take Bailey's mind off Mother's Day, and local matchmaker Kim encourages Iqra to pursue Mila, before turning her attention to Bobby's love life

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Contestants cook their Signature Dish, and after tasting all five plates, judges Gregg Wallace and John Torode award not only a place in the quarter-finals for two participants, but also a MasterChef apron. The remaining trio take part in the Favourite Ingredient Challenge. Gifted with their chosen favourite ingredient, as well as a larder, they are tasked with creating one exceptional dish that showcases it. Arbitrators in this edition include 2015 series champion Simon Wood and finalist Tony Rodd

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Under the eye of the acerbic chef, contestants Sam and Sassan, twin sisters Margaret and Ann and Paul and Stu answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board - the more bars they build up, the more they can win up to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Medical journalist and chronic insomniac Michael Mosley journeys into the world of sleep, finding out what it is and what happens to someone if they do not get enough. Michael seeks to discover whether his own insomnia runs in the family, sending off a blood sample for DNA analysis, and meets Dr Eleanor Scott in Leeds who is doing research into a possible link between Type 2 diabetes and lack sleep. He also looks at possible ways in which people can go about getting a better night's sleep, including a visit to a project in Denmark to find out if a night under the stars can reset his body clock

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A small flat in Tooting, south London, a huge former care home near a large park in Liverpool and a fire-damaged house in Silverdale in Shropshire are all sold at auction. Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin meet the new owners to hear their plans

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers discover an illegal puppy farm in Cannock, putting profit over care. Thirty dogs and two racoons are found kept in filthy conditions, some of them locked in old cars. There's no water, food or in some cases ventilation. Plus, a rogue kitchen trader swindling customers out of thousands of pounds

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: When hearing-impaired Rosie is told her deafness is getting worse, she is desperate to learn sign language. However, she faces opposition from husband Nathan. Written by Charlie Swinbourne, with Sophie Stone and Kelvin Fletcher

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Ochuko Ojiri lead two teams competing at Ardingly Antiques Fair in West Sussex, as they hunt for potentially profitable items to take to auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion monitors their progress and also looks at the art and history of Persian carpets

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles returns with the programme in which three artists are challenged to create pieces for people they have never me, beginning by travelling to Shropshire to meet a buyer who wants to mark her recent return to her roots following a health scare

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Health professionals Hayley and Sunni compete. The pair met on social media over their shared love of a plant-based diet, so in an all-vegan show contest are keen to take the ingredients they cook with all the time and elevate them to new heights. Paired up with chef Romy Gill, Hayley is keen to rustle up some mouth-watering vegan treats in the Green Pepper kitchen. In the Red Tomato kitchen, Sunni is paired with chef Jeremy Pang, who brings his own flair and style to Sunni's usual ingredients. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including an Edwardian shoe closet, a bronze racehorse, a 17th-century writing desk and Concorde service medals. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Six Nations Sin Bin
programme.desc: Gabby Logan and Gareth Thomas gear up for the fourth round of the Six Nations and Wales's game against Italy, alongside guests including MasterChef judge and rugby fan Gregg Wallace, triple Grand Slam-winner Ryan Jones, Australian rugby legend David Campese and media personality Carol Vorderman. Plus, the next round of the fiercely contested, stunt-based rugby competition - Showdown 2021: The Battle of Wales

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The second quarter-final sees four talented heat-winners return to vie for a place in knockout week by participating in the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by William Sitwell, one of the UK's toughest food critics - is to cook a dish inspired by a holiday. Gregg Wallace and John Torode preside as the contenders endeavour to take another step closer to being crowned MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bailey is triumphant as she takes part in the first round of the school science competition - but her joy doesn't last when she returns home to bad news. Lucas is interviewed for a job by Caleb, who isn't fooled by the ex-convict's attempts to pose under a different name - but insists he turn up on Monday or else

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Under the eye of the acerbic chef, contestants Paul and Stu and couple Sophie and Claud answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board - the more bars they build up, the more they can win up to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by comic actress Amy Poehler, star and director of the rebellious new comedy Moxie and Oscar-nominated Hollywood Brit Minnie Driver, who is promoting her new podcast Minnie Questions. Call the Midwife's Leonie Elliott also guests, alongside Hollywood star Jennifer Garner and singer-songwriter Nick Jonas. With music from singer-songwriter Tom Odell

programme.title: Fern Brady: Power & Chaos
programme.desc: In front of a packed crowd in her native Scotland, the rising star delivers an energetic stand-up performance tackling issues of sexuality, feminism, power, Brexit, Britishness, Scottishness and nationality, all with her usual caustic wit and electric stage craft

programme.title: Mum's List (2016)
programme.desc: Young couple Kate and Singe's lives are turned upside down when Kate is diagnosed with an incurable breast cancer. Over the course of her final few months, she creates a list - writing her thoughts and memories down to help the man she loved create the best life possible for their two sons after she is gone. Drama based on the book by St John Greene, starring Rafe Spall, Emilia Fox and Elaine Cassidy

programme.desc: Paul Murton travels through old Perthshire. He lends a hand bringing in the cattle and hears how Rob Roy MacGregor might not have been the romantic figure of popular fiction. Michael seeks to discover whether his own insomnia runs in the family, sending off a blood sample for DNA analysis, and meets Dr Eleanor Scott in Leeds who is doing research into a possible link between Type 2 diabetes and lack sleep. He also looks at possible ways in which people can go about getting a better night's sleep, including a visit to a project in Denmark to find out if a night under the stars can reset his body clock

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland

programme.title: Coronavirus: Scotland's Response
programme.desc: All the latest developments as Scotland responds to the coronavirus pandemic, plus answers to the many questions you have during these unprecedented times

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Health professionals Hayley and Sunni compete. The pair met on social media over their shared love of a plant-based diet, so in an all-vegan show contest are keen to take the ingredients they cook with all the time and elevate them to new heights. Paired up with chef Romy Gill, Hayley is keen to rustle up some mouth-watering vegan treats in the Green Pepper kitchen. programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including an Edwardian shoe closet, a bronze racehorse, a 17th-century writing desk and Concorde service medals. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Lenny struggles to meet Tyler's deadline, even selling businesses to make the shortfall, and for the Murdoch family there's a heavy price to pay. Rebecca tries to hide her disappointment with Angus as Levi gives relationship advice, and Dylan pours his heart out to a sympathetic Ruby

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The second quarter-final sees four talented heat-winners return to vie for a place in knockout week by participating in the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by William Sitwell, one of the UK's toughest food critics - is to cook a dish inspired by a holiday. Gregg Wallace and John Torode preside as the contenders endeavour to take another step closer to being crowned MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bailey is triumphant as she takes part in the first round of the school science competition - but her joy doesn't last when she returns home to bad news. Lucas is interviewed for a job by Caleb, who isn't fooled by the ex-convict's attempts to pose under a different name - but insists he turn up on Monday or else

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Under the eye of the acerbic chef, contestants Paul and Stu and couple Sophie and Claud answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board - the more bars they build up, the more they can win up to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by comic actress Amy Poehler, star and director of the rebellious new comedy Moxie and Oscar-nominated Hollywood Brit Minnie Driver, who is promoting her new podcast Minnie Questions. programme.title: Fern Brady: Power & Chaos
programme.desc: In front of a packed crowd in her native Scotland, the rising star delivers an energetic stand-up performance tackling issues of sexuality, feminism, power, Brexit, Britishness, Scottishness and nationality, all with her usual caustic wit and electric stage craft

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain hurdling great Colin Jackson, former England striker Darren Bent, ex-WBA Super Middleweight champion boxer George Groves and Commonwealth Games gold medal-winning swimmer Aimee Willmott join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: An irreverent look at the world of Scottish Football, hosted by Craig G Telfer. Contributors including Joel Sked, Shaughan McGuigan, Craig Fowler and Robert Borthwick dissect the ins and outs of the Scottish footballing week just passed, offering analysis and opinion in their own unique way

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel becomes increasingly embroiled in the university hunger strike, despite Zara warning him to stay away. When teenager Rosie's own mother starts treating her like a stranger, she realises she is locked out of a family secret

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: A divorcee finds a new lease of life when his daughter buys him a course of fitness sessions for his 65th birthday. However, his new hobby threatens to drive a wedge between him and his close-knit group of friends. Starring Les Dennis and Liz McClarnon, with Aaron Cobham, Louis Emerick and Michael Starke

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple with a budget of £600,000 search for a rural home in the Lancashire countryside, and later Ginny Buckley visits the Victorian seaside resort of Blackpool

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Birmingham mother and son Julie and Rich take to the kitchen - she to push the boundaries and discover new flavours, he to learn new ideas to impress his vegan girlfriend

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on an art deco cigarette case, a wooden bureau, a china sheepdog by Beswick and a Clarice Cliff preserve bowl. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Barra on the Foyle
programme.desc: Barra Best continues his journey along the Foyle river network, taking him along three counties - Donegal, Tyrone and Londonderry, to Lough Foyle. Here he joins a group practising ice water therapy on the River Roe and gets an icy surprise when his instructor decides the river is not cold enough. He also follows the journey of the salmon up Donegal's River Finn and visits the Co Tyrone town of Strabane where he meets some of those forced to flee their homes the night the River Mourne burst its banks

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal maestro Dominic Chinea gets to work on a crumbling cast-iron garden gate, whose design includes the badge of the Royal Lancers, while horologist Steve fletcher and leather expert Suzie Fletcher join up to restore a wristwatch awarded for bravery in the First World War. Upholsterer Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on a rocking chair that has been at the heart of a family home for decades, and Geoff Harvey is given a 1920s penny arcade game

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Gordon welcomes two more pairs - partners Caroline and Andrew, and brothers Sam and Sassan. Will either duo have what it takes to beat the board and walk away with the jackpot of £100,000?

programme.title: The Top Table
programme.desc: An audience of young people face down our politicians in the topical debate show, presented by Stephen Nolan

programme.title: Zara McDermott: Revenge Porn
programme.desc: Zara McDermott shot to fame on Love Island in 2018. But as soon as she left the villa, Zara was told that naked photos she had shared with an ex-lover had started circulating worldwide. The incident left Zara devastated, ashamed and looking for answers. Here she sets out to understand more about the impact of revenge porn on victims and questions whether anyone can safely share intimate images again

programme.title: Catch Her If You Can
programme.desc: Documentary following an attempt to expose a successful London businesswoman's double life as a con artist. A suspicious invoice tipped off a potential victim, and inspired her to track down other parties who had been fleeced in the past. Together, they came up with a plan to expose the woman's misdeeds

programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph visits a detached house in Bourne in Lincolnshire, Martel Maxwell takes a look around a mid-terrace in Walsall in the West Midlands and Martin Roberts explores a former library in Troedyrhiw, south Wales. They then witness all three properties going under auction before talking to the buyers about their plans for their acquisitions

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow

programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Tim Weeks provide advice to two teams competing at Wadebridge, Cornwall, while host Anita Manning hops on a steam train at Bodmin Railway to find out more about the Beeching cuts to the rail network

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel fears for Anita, Zara is upset about Emma and Luca's newfound spark and Colin intends to declare his love to his sugar baby - but Karen thinks he is making a mistake

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: A gifted, but neglected, teenager's education is jeopardised when she and her mother are made homeless. Ashamed of her plight, she struggles to find help, all the while concerned that she may not be able to study for A levels after all. Written by Lisa McMullin, with Tillie Amartey and Mina Anwar

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin goes house-hunting in the south Wales countryside with an itinerant couple who have £600,000 to find a rural retreat close to their growing grandchildren. While in the area, Jonnie heads to the south coast of Monmouthshire, and tries his hand at catching a salmon using the rare art of lave net fishing, a traditional skill which stretches back 400 years

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Husband-and-wife duo Faraj and Chloe are paired with chefs Ellis Barrie and Akis Petretzikis, and soon put to work in their respective kitchens. It's a fast-and-furious cook with both Faraj and Chloe keen to come out victorious. But it's all very much in the balance when the 20 Minute cook is over and the 10 Minute Challenge ingredients are revealed. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against each other to buy four new items - a vintage Steiff teddy bear, a two-headed Georgian nutcracker, a very special bottle of Chateau Palmer wine, and a retro mechanical tin toy. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Karen goes on a date with mystery man 'Jordan' - unaware she is sitting down for a drink with Lucas - and is so excited she takes a sneaky picture of him to show her family. Jay and Billy argue over who will take Janet to her audition - only for an accident to leave the girl stranded. Isaac comes up with an idea to help take Bailey's mind off Mother's Day, and local matchmaker Kim encourages Iqra to pursue Mila, before turning her attention to Bobby's love life

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Contestants cook their Signature Dish, and after tasting all five plates, judges Gregg Wallace and John Torode award not only a place in the quarter-finals for two participants, but also a MasterChef apron. The remaining trio take part in the Favourite Ingredient Challenge. Gifted with their chosen favourite ingredient, as well as a larder, they are tasked with creating one exceptional dish that showcases it. Arbitrators in this edition include 2015 series champion Simon Wood and finalist Tony Rodd

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance

programme.title: I Can't Say My Name: Stammering in the Spotlight
programme.desc: BBC producer Felicity Baker has a stammer that she has spent her whole life trying to hide. She struggles to say her own name. Now she is stepping out in front of the camera for the first time to talk about what it is like to live with the condition - and she soon finds she is not alone. She meets a rugby player who blames his stammer for his aggressive behaviour on the pitch, a rapper who found music stopped him stammering and the comedy star Michael Palin, whose father stammered but never ever spoke about it

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: As the Covid-19 pandemic grips the nation, cameras follow life at Heathrow, as depleted staff members fight to keep the airport going. Customer service team member Demi is on hand to offer help to all those who are allowed to travel in and out of the airport, and has his work cut out as he tries to track down a missing passenger. Elsewhere, staff adjust to the new safety measures put in place to stop the spread of the virus

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Peter encourages Carla to follow her heart. Sean is taken in by Rhydian's flattery. Corey voices his disgust at Asha and Nina's relationship

programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Eight celebrities prepare to embark on a life changing journey to explore their family history using DNA technology and genealogy. This time, Jamie Redknapp and Freddie Flintoff travel around the country to discover each other's family histories. Proud Lancastrian Freddie is anxious that his DNA may lead him to Yorkshire, and there is a surprise in store when the pair arrive the Wakefield Mining Museum. Later, Jamie's family journey takes him to London's East End, where he uncovers a notorious mystery that has remained unsolved for generations

programme.desc: Twenty five years ago, the small Scottish town of Dunblane was struck by tragedy when 16 primary school children and their teacher were murdered by a lone gunman. Lorraine Kelly was a reporter on the ground that day, and in this documentary she returns to speak again to those affected by the events. Her film also looks at the spirit of the town, which showed remarkable resilience and mutual support in the aftermath of the deadliest mass shooting in British history

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: As the Covid-19 pandemic grips the nation, cameras follow life at Heathrow, as depleted staff members fight to keep the airport going. programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Eight celebrities prepare to embark on a life changing journey to explore their family history using DNA technology and genealogy. This time, Jamie Redknapp and Freddie Flintoff travel around the country to discover each other's family histories. Proud Lancastrian Freddie is anxious that his DNA may lead him to Yorkshire, and there is a surprise in store when the pair arrive the Wakefield Mining Museum. Later, Jamie's family journey takes him to London's East End, where he uncovers a notorious mystery that has remained unsolved for generations

programme.title: Return to Dunblane with Lorraine Kelly
programme.desc: Twenty five years ago, the small Scottish town of Dunblane was struck by tragedy when 16 primary school children and their teacher were murdered by a lone gunman. Lorraine Kelly was a reporter on the ground that day, and in this documentary she returns to speak again to those affected by the events. Her film also looks at the spirit of the town, which showed remarkable resilience and mutual support in the aftermath of the deadliest mass shooting in British history

Plus, Peter Stringfellow uses a paintball gun to fire Viagra, Harry reveals the real reason Zayn left One Direction and Caitlin Moran falls off a catamaran. Narrated by Harry Hill

programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Recruitment manager Neale from Northampton, fire training officer Will from London, banker Johnny from Glasgow, and holiday-camp entertainer Ben from Essex all enter Paddy McGuinness's 'love lift'. programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Klaus is diagnosed with fish cough, which is has a 99 per cent mortality rate. There is a life-saving surgery available, but it's too expensive and Stan refuses to pay for it. programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre tells Liz she gives off the wrong impression to men. Judy is touched when Rita opens a savings account for Katie. Des is furious when his house is vandalised again

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Zoe assures the Malletts she hasn't come to cause trouble. Jack is horrified when Renee tracks him down. Sally tells Rita that she's been seeing Chris

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris suggests to Kathy that she comes to stay with him at Home Farm. Zak and Lisa argue about Butch. Donna pesters Andy to find out why he's been acting strangely

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andy steals some money from the post office. Rachel cannot forgive or forget Kathy's insults. Mandy tells Paddy that Butch is a liar

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Village life is disrupted by a circus family who are accused of corrupting the vicar's daughter, wrecking the shop and breaking into the church. Meanwhile, Nick and Kate are attacked for resisting local bigots. Eleanor Bron guest stars, with Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: When his boss goes on holiday, Sid assumes responsibility for guarding his property - but has trouble enforcing his regime. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Owing to a little white lie on his income tax returns, Mr Roper must quickly acquire a son. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick is seconded to a Scotland Yard team who have arrived in Aidensfield to investigate the fatal shooting of former policeman Andrew Gerard, and risks his career to ensure justice is done. Meanwhile, local man Alan Maskell is duped into helping a couple of crooks respray stolen cars. Nick Berry stars

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Butch is full of remorse after attacking Marlon. Billy enlists Andy's help to steal more money. Chris is shocked to see Eric competing against him at the auction

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lisa wants Zak to invite Marlon to Christmas dinner so they can all make a fresh start. Billy loads his shotgun ready for his Christmas Day robbery plan

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Malletts look forward to their new life in Newcastle. Chris tells Sally he wants to move in with her. Des reluctantly thanks Les for coming to his rescue

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natalie wants Kevin to find out about Sally and Chris. Alec asks Jack to escort for him with a new client. Maxine is stunned when Audrey tells her she intends to buy the salon

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As the sleuth studies for his forthcoming sergeant's exam, he investigates a hit-and-run that claimed the life of an eminent Oxford professor. The victim had been at odds with his peers over the fate of a potentially lucrative parcel of college-owned land, and the case is complicated by the appearance of DI Thursday's old gangland enemy - whom he must face alone while Endeavour makes a trip back to Lincolnshire to visit his ailing father

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: When the Metropolitan Police discover the burglary after the long Easter weekend, the gang members already have a two-day lead on them. They are desperate to split up the stolen goods, but more delays frustrate them. However, they soon come to realise that it is not only the police who are chasing them - Brian Reader also wants his share

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: A gangland killing takes a surprising twist when it turns out the victim has been sexually mutilated. Andy punishes Janet by keeping her out of the heart of the investigation, and when she rebels, the ensuing row becomes personal. Rachel feels ambushed by Sean, who announces his mother wants to meet her - especially as she has spent years trying not to think about her own mum. Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Sean Maguire and Nicholas Gleaves star

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack returns to duty after a period of suspension, and finds himself investigating the murder of a man who had been leading a double life. In addition, he also has to deal with the baffling case of a man found dead with his right foot hacked off and replaced by a severed left foot. Drama, starring David Jason and Amanda Root

programme.desc: Ashley pleads with Maxine to return home. The Underworld girls have their Christmas party in the Rovers. Samantha gets sick of Les making suggestive remarks towards her

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When psychologist Reuben Beatty is murdered while moonlighting as a psychic, Lewis and Hathaway struggle to unravel the victim's baffling double life. Dark forces appear to be at work, and not just in the afterlife. Sanjeev Bhaskar guest stars, with Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox, Clare Holman and Rebecca Front

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: With the surveillance teams hot on their tails, the gang members squabble among themselves over who should receive a cut of the takings. Having performed such a professional raid, they are now behaving like amateurs, and with tensions running high, Perkins breaks his own golden rule by organising another meeting. Will he cut Reader in on the deal? Timothy Spall, Kenneth Cranham and David Hayman star in the fact-based drama

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel discovers Nick has been brutally assaulted - and she is top of the list of suspects. But even worse, she has no recollection of the previous night after a drunken hen party, and struggles to convince herself she did not do it. Janet thinks she has made her peace with Andy, until he gives her some misinformation that results in Gill missing an appointment with the coroner - so the DCI, tired of their games, announces one of them will have to go. A man's accidental death turns into a murder investigation when his daughters reveal their mother often talked of killing him. Crime drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Downtrodden detective Jack Frost investigates after an unidentified man's body is found on top of a pile of glass at a council recycling centre - and the subsequent autopsy reveals a few surprises. Another case is soon on the cards when an apparent suicide raises suspicions. On the personal front, Frost decides to get in shape and joins a gym, where he falls for the charms of his fitness trainer. Starring David Jason and Claire Cox

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andy learns the truth about the robbery. Viv talks to Alan about going to identify Vic. The Dingles wonder what to call the baby

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andy pleads with his dad to give himself up. Mandy and Paddy make friends again. Kelly tells Scott as far as she is concerned their kiss never happened

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Zoe becomes hysterical at the thought of the Malletts taking Katie away. Kevin resolves to win Sally back. Ashley feels caught in the middle between Zoe and the Malletts

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The master of a prestigious college asks Morse to investigate the disappearance of one of his colleagues. The subsequent discovery of a dismembered torso soon has everyone fearing the worst - until the missing man turns up alive and well. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, James Grout, Beatie Edney, Sian Thomas and Barry Foster

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor and Louisa make plans for their wedding, but the big day runs far from smoothly, as a drunken vicar leaves Martin with a dilemma. Meanwhile, the restaurant where the reception is to be held is flooded and Isobel goes into labour. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Former nanny Lavinia Seagram is found soaked in blood and it is believed she had turned to drink and drugs to commit suicide. However, Poirot is soon on the scent of troubled heiress Norma Restarick, who had problems of her own - not least confessing to the possibility she has killed someone herself. Guest starring Jemima Rooper, Zoe Wanamaker and Peter Bowles, with David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Called to the home of an elderly couple who haven't been seen for a while, Janet discovers Eunice Bevan's severed head at the bottom of the stairs, her body at the top, and her husband Joe in bed, emaciated and barely able to speak. Meanwhile, the detective puts her house on the market, admitting her relationship with Ade is over, and three months in to Rachel's marriage to traffic cop Sean, she's starting to feel a little claustrophobic. George Costigan and Nicola Walker guest star, with Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The irascible copper finds his methods are called into question when a colleague is seriously injured during an attempt to thwart a suicide. The murder of a widow soon gives Frost a chance to prove his worth again, but the investigation only reveals an intriguing web of deceit, and it isn't until a forensic psychologist joins the team that they realise a serial killer is at work. With David Jason, Philip Jackson, Jeff Rawle and Danny Webb

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Owing to a little white lie on his income tax returns, Mr Roper must quickly acquire a son. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As the sleuth studies for his forthcoming sergeant's exam, he investigates a hit-and-run that claimed the life of an eminent Oxford professor. The victim had been at odds with his peers over the fate of a potentially lucrative parcel of college-owned land, and the case is complicated by the appearance of DI Thursday's old gangland enemy - whom he must face alone while Endeavour makes a trip back to Lincolnshire to visit his ailing father

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: When the Metropolitan Police discover the burglary after the long Easter weekend, the gang members already have a two-day lead on them. They are desperate to split up the stolen goods, but more delays frustrate them. However, they soon come to realise that it is not only the police who are chasing them - Brian Reader also wants his share

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The daughter of a prominent professor is terrified by a mysterious prowler who haunts the grounds of her home by night and seems able to scale sheer walls with ease. Guest starring Charles Kay, Sarah Woodward, Adrian Lukis and Alice Morphy

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar is persuaded to shadow a defecting civil servant who has been duped into carrying confidential papers to East Germany. Guest starring Ronald Hines

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter discover a road accident victim was a gang member involved in a wages robbery, and Regan is determined to nail the modern-day Bonnie and Clyde who have already made fools of the police. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: With whole weeks missing from his life and evidence of actions that he simply cannot remember, Steed finds himself in an endless series of deja vu situations. In reality he is slowly being hypnotised and the aim is to mesmerise him into killing 'Mother'. Kate O'Mara guest stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur and Ray's dreams of riches Down Under fall apart. Left penniless, Ray is forced to bar work while Arthur encounters roguish street trader Derek Collins. George Cole and Gary Webster star

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: Discover how the famous Henry vacuum cleaner is made, and go behind the scenes at the family firm churning out dairy ice cream. Narrated by Jimmy Nail

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: An elderly widow enlists Holmes' help to ascertain why a mystery purchaser should want to buy not just her house, but also all its contents. Drama, starring Jeremy Brett, Edward Hardwicke and Peter Wyngarde

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar sets out on the trail of a former Nazi believed to have taken refuge in South America. Vintage drama, guest starring Francesca Annis, Roger Delgado, John Barrie and Maurice Kaufmann

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A business tycoon is blackmailed for his part in a massacre while serving in the Army 25 years earlier. However, his problems are compounded after he seeks help from his former commanding officer. Colin Jeavons guest stars, with John Thaw

programme.title: The Avengers

programme.title: River Monsters

programme.desc: A former Air Force test pilot, who was sidelined from space exploration because of his difficult character, is recruited 40 years later to help America's post-Cold War Russian allies. With Tommy Lee Jones, James Garner, Donald Sutherland and James Cromwell

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: The world teeters on the brink of war as an ingenious mastermind manages to frame Miss King to look like a traitor as part of his plan to trigger a major conflict. Classic fantasy spy drama, starring Patrick Macnee and Linda Thorson

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur attempts to protect his friend Sidney, who on mistakenly being diagnosed with a fatal illness, arranged for a hitman to kill him so his wife would benefit from his life insurance. While Ray attempts to counter the deal, the best safe house the dodgy dealer can provide is a leaky caravan in Brighton, the property of Dave's sister. Andrew Sachs guest stars, with George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.desc: How malt vinegar is brewed, how Aga stoves are made to last a lifetime and a chance to see the work taking place at Britain's last remaining tennis ball factory

programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at two bungalows requiring different solutions. In Larne in Northern Ireland, Catherine and Nigel Finch have hit a spot of turbulence due to their dated, 1970s-style home. programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Cornish adaptive surfer Pegleg plans to extend his VW campervan by creating a new cabin from scratch, while Martin and Carol-Anne want to build a glamping pod in their Pembrokeshire garden, using a dome that originally housed an electricity generator at the London Olympics. George also visits an earthquake-resistant glass house on the coast of western Japan, and looks at aluminium panels as a new kind of canopy for his treehouse

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Environmental journalist Lucy Siegle investigates the rise of waste incineration in the UK, examining how millions of tonnes of waste left out for recycling ends up being burned. The rise in carbon emissions from incineration is put under the spotlight, with exclusive research revealing that it is on course to become the UK's dirtiest form of energy production. The programme also looks at how councils are locked into expensive contracts with incinerator operators forcing them to burn waste for decades to come

programme.desc: Norm's boozing expertise secures him his dream job as chief beer-taster at a brewery, while Rebecca grows addicted to the bar's new slot machine. George Wendt stars alongside Ted Danson

programme.desc: Carrie is invited to a company gathering, where Doug impresses the other lawyers' partners with tales of his fictitious mountaineering exploits. Back at home, Arthur wrecks their bedroom and enlists Deacon's help in repairing the damage before they return

programme.desc: A classy restaurant awards the shrink a place on its wall of fame, but he takes exception to the caricature of himself and tries to persuade the head waiter to replace it with a more flattering likeness. Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce star

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Daphne's brother turns up at the wedding, bringing nothing with him but an unquenchable thirst for alcohol and a squadron of drinking pals. programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part one of two. The shrink proffers romantic advice to both Niles and Daphne, hoping that they will admit their mutual affection. However, it remains to be seen whether they will have the nerve to be honest with each other before her impending marriage to Donny

programme.desc: Randy DeWitt, the CEO and co-founder of bar and grill chain Twin Peaks, goes undercover to find out whether the company's culture is keeping up with its rapid growth. Posing as a contestant on a reality TV competition, he works among his own staff

Dad couldn't stand him back then and hid in his shed, but will they get along this time? Meanwhile, Jim collects money for charity dressed in a dog costume that doesn't leave out any detail. Thriller, starring Anna Hutchison and Mark Lawson

programme.desc: There are complications when a woman who suffers from kidney failure is admitted with a heart condition, and two builders are rushed into casualty after a roof collapsed under them. programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Dipi is forced to face the consequences of her actions. Hendrix, unable to take a hint, arranges another horribly misguided romantic gesture towards Harlow

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The stories of six epic train journeys, seen through the characters who travel, work and live near them. The first programme rides through Canada's iconic Rocky Mountaineer train in a jaw-dropping loop from Vancouver to the heart of the Rockies, through some of North America's most staggering natural beauty, and on to Banff in Alberta. The journey also ventures through the spiral tunnels and shows how to track bears with the National Park rangers. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Documentary featuring exclusive access to Nottinghamshire's traffic units. The firearms team mobilises to tackle a suspect armed with a pistol, and hours into a nerve-shredding siege, the man emerges with a handgun and fires. Elsewhere, interceptors Rob and Dan join in the pursuit of a runaway hatchback, while the members of the knife crime team track down a suspect on a bike trying to evade capture in a park

programme.title: The Killing of James Bulger
programme.desc: On 12th February 1993, a CCTV camera captured footage of two young boys leading a two-year-old child away from the Strand shopping centre in Liverpool. This two-part documentary tells the definitive story of the case, examining in forensic detail what happened on that tragic day, how the young killers were caught and the devastating legacy of the murder on James's family and the community. With access to his mother Denise Fergus, his brothers speaking exclusively for the first time, along with the police and others closely involved in the case

programme.title: Medical Mysteries
programme.desc: Documentary featuring more patients in search of answers for their mysterious conditions. Alicia was in her first term at university when she started to suffer uncontrollable tremors, making it impossible for her to walk, talk and write. Nineteen-year-old Elizabeth's body changed dramatically within 18 months as her weight almost doubled and she developed facial hair and a hump on her back, while air stewardess Dee had to leave her job of 25 years when she developed an unexplained sickness

programme.title: Dale Cregan: Hunting a Cop Killer
programme.desc: Documentary looking back at the hunt for the serial killer Dale Cregan in Manchester in 2012, who killed four people, two of which were female police officers. After murdering two men linked to a rival family in quick succession, Cregan lured two innocent female officers to his house in Hattersley, Tameside, where he shot them multiple times before fleeing from the scene and eventually handing himself in to the police

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Nick Wallis examines how CCTV means wrongdoers can no longer hide in a crowd, looking at its use in convicting people involved in the August 2011 riots across England

programme.title: Kate Humble's Coastal Britain
programme.desc: Starting at Dunwich Heath, Kate walks an 11-mile stretch of the Suffolk coastline - one of the fastest eroding in Europe. The naturalist also finds one of the country's biggest freshwater reed beds, and learns about the ancient art of thatching

programme.title: Gregg Wallace: Big Weekends Away
programme.desc: The presenter heads to Italy and reveals it is possible to enjoy Venice without breaking the bank. By avoiding the tourist traps he sees a new side to the city. Over three days, Gregg explores one of Italy's most distinct food cultures, explores the city's fascinating and surprising history, and meets the locals who make Venice so special

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 80s Songs
programme.desc: Janice Long recalls the hits of 1982, when ABC, Culture Club, Musical Youth, Tears for Fears, the Stranglers, Kraftwerk, the Jam, Yazoo, and the Goombay Dance Band made their way up the charts. Toyah Willcox, Paul Gambaccini, Cheryl Baker and Clare Grogan add their thoughts on the era

programme.title: That's So
programme.desc: A look back at 1982, featuring news, pop and TV archive clips. It was a year that saw the Falklands War, the birth of Prince William, Channel 4's launch together with the first episode of Countdown, and Steven Spielberg's ET: The Extra-Terrestrial break box office records. It was also the age of new romanticism and electropop, while tracks such as Come on Eileen, Eye of the Tiger and Pass the Dutchie stormed the charts

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica is visited by a cousin she has not seen since childhood, whose stunning looks do not go unnoticed by Chandler. Rachel and Phoebe plan a last-minute bridal party, while Joey tries to trick his way into an acting role. Guest starring Denise Richards

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Drivers head out one last time during the spring thaw, Steph Custance slides into trees, while Todd Dewey struggles with a load on a twisty trail. Elsewhere, Art Burke retrieves a heavy bulldozer, Reno Ward's truck dies on the ice and Lisa Kelly receives an emergency call

programme.title: Building Britain's Canals
programme.desc: Dan Jones examines Britain's most iconic canals, beginning with the 152-mile Grand Union Canal, which runs up the spine of the country. Constructed at the height of the Industrial Revolution, the task challenged even the greatest engineering minds of the time, including pioneers such as James Brindley, the man behind Britain's first commercial canal, and William Jessop, the person with whom responsibility lay for the building of the main link from Birmingham to London. Travelling along its route, Dan experiences first-hand the innovations behind its construction, including the Blisworth tunnel

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Property expert Ann Maurice visits the Leicestershire village of Quorn to see a two-bedroom cottage that has been on the market for four months despite its picturesque location. Priorities include making the most of the cramped space by clearing out accumulated clutter and harmonising clashing colour schemes

programme.title: Pony Express (1953)
programme.desc: Buffalo Bill Cody and Wild Bill Hickok work on establishing an east-west route for the pioneering mail service that will allow messages to be sent from Missouri to California in 10 days. However, they have to contend with saboteurs planning to prevent California becoming part of the Union. Western, starring Charlton Heston, Forrest Tucker, Rhonda Fleming and Jan Sterling

programme.title: The Long Memory (1952)
programme.desc: A man is released from prison after serving a 12-year sentence for a crime he did not commit, and immediately sets about tracking down three witnesses who lied in court, bringing about his conviction. However, while seeking revenge, he discovers the case is more complicated than expected. Thriller, starring John Mills, Elizabeth Sellars, John McCallum and Geoffrey Keen

programme.title: Detective Story (1951)
programme.desc: A moralistic New York cop is determined to ensure the conviction of an illegal abortionist implicated in the deaths of several women. However, his obsession leads him to lose control, and he is disturbed to discover his own wife was once one of the doctor's patients - a secret she had kept from him. Police drama, starring Kirk Douglas, Eleanor Parker and William Bendix

programme.title: Apache Uprising (1965)
programme.desc: Western, starring Rory Calhoun as a tough-talking cowboy with a heart of gold who bravely defends fellow stagecoach passengers from a potentially lethal Indian attack. Also starring Corinne Calvet, Lon Chaney Jnr and John Russell

programme.title: Home Alone (1990)
programme.desc: A boy has to fend for himself when he is accidentally left behind after his family jets off to Paris for Christmas. The youngster initially enjoys his freedom, but is soon forced to resort to ingenious booby traps to defend the family home from two dim-witted burglars. Comedy, starring Macaulay Culkin, Joe Pesci, Daniel Stern, John Heard, Catherine O'Hara and John Candy

programme.title: Taken (2008)
programme.desc: An American teenager travelling through Europe is abducted by a gang of sex traffickers. However, what the kidnappers do not realise is that the girl's father is a former CIA agent, who employs all his old skills as he searches for the criminals and tries to rescue his daughter. Thriller, starring Liam Neeson, Famke Janssen and Maggie Grace

programme.title: Cowboys & Aliens (2011)
programme.desc: A Wild-West town dominated by a cattle baron comes under attack by invaders from another world. It falls to a mysterious stranger with no memory to unite the townsfolk, a gang of outlaws and an Apache tribe, in a bid to fight back against the aliens, and learn the truth about his own past. Sci-fi Western, with Daniel Craig, Harrison Ford, Olivia Wilde and Sam Rockwell

programme.title: Eden (2014)
programme.desc: A literature student abandons his thesis as his DJing career takes off, achieving success in US clubs, but his spending and cocaine habit start catching up with him. Drama, starring Felix de Givry and Pauline Etienne. In French and English

programme.title: Dakota Incident (1956)
programme.desc: Mismatched travellers, including a bank robber, a politician and a dancing girl, are targeted by Cheyenne braves when their stagecoach is ambushed in the Dakota desert. When the coach is set on fire, they take cover in a gully, and a drawn-out conflict ensues. Western, starring Dale Robertson, Linda Darnell, John Lund and Ward Bond

programme.title: Run Silent, Run Deep (1958)
programme.desc: A commander aboard a US Navy submarine has a titanic clash of egos with his second in command as the senior officer's obsession with sinking as many enemy vessels as possible, whatever the risk, spirals out of control. Second World War drama, starring Clark Gable, Burt Lancaster, Jack Warden, Brad Dexter and Don Rickles

programme.title: Thunder Bay (1953)
programme.desc: A former Navy engineer comes up with a plan to establish an offshore oil drilling site on the Louisiana coast. However, his scheme is threatened by dangerous weather conditions, his own treacherous financial backers, and irate locals who fear for their fishing business. Action adventure, with James Stewart and Joanne Dru

programme.title: Carry On Teacher (1959)
programme.desc: A headmaster hopes to land a more prestigious job at a better school, but his pupils do not want him to leave. When two inspectors pay a visit, the mischievous students see a chance to sabotage his career plans with a series of pranks. Comedy, starring Ted Ray, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey and Kenneth Williams

programme.title: Stargate (1994)
programme.desc: An Egyptologist and a soldier head an expedition through a mysterious portal to an alien planet. The world they find resembles ancient Egypt and is under the rule of a tyrant who has convinced the downtrodden inhabitants that he is a god - until the humans incite the enslaved populace to fight back. Sci-fi adventure, starring Kurt Russell, James Spader and Jaye Davidson

programme.title: xXx: The Return of Xander Cage (2017)
programme.desc: The extreme sports-loving super spy is brought out of exile when a lethal gang of mercenaries steal a powerful new weapon that threatens the safety of the entire world. He must assemble his own team of adrenaline junkie agents to recover the lethal device. Action thriller, starring Vin Diesel, Donnie Yen, Toni Collette and Samuel L Jackson

programme.title: Split (2016)
programme.desc: After being abducted by a kidnapper with multiple personalities, three young women are soon engaged in a fight for survival. Meanwhile, an oblivious psychiatrist tries to ease her patient's fractured mind as a 24th character with apparent superhuman abilities attempts to dominate his body. M Night Shyamalan's thriller, starring James McAvoy, Anya Taylor-Joy and Betty Buckley

programme.title: Hyena (2014)
programme.desc: A corrupt cop with access to all the money and drugs he needs suddenly finds his comfortable way of life turned upside down by the arrival of ruthless Albanian gangsters in his patch of London. Crime thriller, starring Peter Ferdinando, Stephen Graham and Neil Maskell

programme.title: The Stranger (1946)
programme.desc: A war crimes investigator is on the trail of an infamous Nazi. Drama, directed by and starring Orson Welles, with Loretta Young

programme.title: Two Rode Together (1961)
programme.desc: The desperate relatives of people captured by Comanches many years before put pressure on the US military to bring them home. The army hires a cynical frontier marshal, who agrees to negotiate their release in return for a commission. But the townsfolk are forced to face some grim truths when he returns with just one unrecognisable captive. John Ford's Western, with James Stewart, Richard Widmark and Shirley Jones

programme.title: The Longest Day (1962)
programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War drama chronicling the planning and execution of the Allied landings in Normandy on June 6, 1944, from the differing perspectives of the British, German, French and American forces. Starring Richard Burton, Richard Todd, Kenneth More, Sean Connery, John Wayne, Robert Mitchum, Henry Fonda, Curt Jurgens, Roddy McDowall and Rod Steiger

programme.title: Night at the Museum: Secret of the Tomb (2014)
programme.desc: Nightwatchman Larry notices that the living exhibits of his museum are behaving strangely. He discovers that the magical artefact that brings them to life is damaged, so he and his friends travel to London's Natural History Museum to find a way to restore it. Fantasy comedy sequel, starring Ben Stiller, Rebel Wilson and Robin Williams in his final film role

programme.title: The Last Stand (2013)
programme.desc: Disgraced narcotics cop Ray Owens retires into a quiet life as sheriff of a small town on the Mexican border. He gets the chance to prove his worth as a law enforcer when a drug lord escapes from prison and tries to flee the US - with his escape route taking him through Ray's jurisdiction. Action thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Johnny Knoxville and Forest Whitaker

programme.title: Morgan (2016)
programme.desc: A corporate risk-management consultant decides whether or not to destroy an artificially created humanoid, who was at the centre of a disturbing accident and could pose a potential threat to the human race - or a fantastic opportunity. Sci-fi thriller, starring Kate Mara, Anya Taylor-Joy, Rose Leslie and Michelle Yeoh

programme.desc: Two estranged sisters are reunited when their parents decide to sell the house they grew up in.
