programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of Brighton couple Daren Howarth and Adi Nortje, who want to build an earth-sheltered home from recycled materials, based on ideas pioneered in New Mexico in the 1970s. However, land prices and planning laws have forced the pair out of England and into Brittany. To keep costs down, Daren and Adi enlist the help of unskilled volunteers to work on the house, in exchange for teaching them the construction techniques

programme.title: The Great British Dig: History in Your Back Garden
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis and the archaeologists set out to uncover evidence of a secret military base on the clifftops of South Shields, hopping to become the first to put these important pieces of wartime history on the map

programme.title: Meghan & Harry: The Baby Years
programme.desc: IIn a programme originally shown in 2019, Sunday Times royal correspondent Roya Nikkhah examines what the future might hold for the Duke and Duchess of Sussex and their son Archie

programme.title: Jon Snow's Very Hard Questions
programme.desc: So Far Solent Good and Picture Perfect go head to head as they face tough questions on topics including science, sport and history. Hosted by Jon Snow

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Miles Jupp and Sophie Duker join Sean Lock and Jon Richardson for the words-and-numbers game, while Stevenage builders Lee and Dean joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A woman who has taken drugs and is hallucinating causes a challenge at a hotel, and a worried mother rings for help when her child is feverish and unable to speak

programme.title: The Great British Dig: History in Your Back Garden
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis and the archaeologists set out to uncover evidence of a secret military base on the clifftops of South Shields, hopping to become the first to put these important pieces of wartime history on the map

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton investigates malt, revealing how one of the unsung heroes of the kitchen cupboard is produced, while Jimmy Doherty follows the trail from Russia to Ramsgate to discover why caviar is so expensive and Matt Tebbutt examines what goes into muesli

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp teams up with potter Richard Miller to help Ellie and Nick Harrington - who have no practical DIY skills - create plates to complement their 1930s-style dining room in Tunbridge Wells, Kent

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Mark and Maggie have been together for 35 years and are looking for a holiday home in Barbados, where they can escape British winters and make the most of the tropical climate. With a budget of £350,000, Jasmine Harman uses her extensive knowledge of the island to find the best properties for the pair to choose between

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel around Britain to find items to fix up and flog on to make a profit for their owners. In the Cotswolds, the duo compete to see who can create the most profit on two old lamps and take a spin in a Devonshire fire engine

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel around Britain to find items to fix up and flog on to make a profit for their owners. In this episode, Henry finds a treat for restorer Guy, and Simon creates stunning table lamps from old theatre lights

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel expand the project by setting up a luxury campsite on the chateau grounds, but they only have six weeks to complete it before the first guests arrive - and the weather is not on their side, with constant rain turning the planned site into a muddy field. They also have to contend with a visit from Dick's family at Easter

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: In the south Wales countryside, Jodie and Rhodri Howells want to build a contemporary family home but can't decide what to do with the 1950s bungalow that's currently on their site. Charlie Luxton guides them through the pros and cons, to help them achieve the look they want on a modest budget

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The latest competition kicks off at The Somerset House Hotel in Weymouth, run by couple Adam Borton and Holly Syme, who hope their boutique-style rooms and BBQ breakfast will whet their guests' appetite. Guests Sam and Mandy are blown away by their surroundings, but Belinda and Brenda aren't fans of the noise and find the sofa bed to be a bad bunk. In the morning, the breakfast gets a grilling and Sandeep and Vaishalee's revelations leave the group with high expectations

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to the seaside town of Dawlish in Devon, where the owner of the Knitting Hotel, Belinda Harris-Reid, and her colleague Brenda Watson hope that their guests will get excited about all things woolly. Most of the guests embrace the hotel's theme, but the minimal feel to the rooms leaves Adam and Holly berating the lack of the basics

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit of the week the group heads to Stade Court Hotel in Hythe in Kent, where owners Sandeep and Vaishalee Chawla have a hosting policy of treating their guests like a god. However, some guests have cleanliness concerns, with Holly and Adam having to call in the management

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mandy McCready and her assistant Sam Gould welcome their rivals to the Goodlife Guest House, a family business in Harwich in Essex. As their guests pull into the car park, the location is met with raised eyebrows but the B&Bers are pleasantly surprised when they see their rooms. During an afternoon of crabbing on Harwich pier, Sandeep and Vaishalee reveal that they're still struggling to shake off Belinda's feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to settle some scores, with Adam Borton and Holly Syme from the Somerset House Hotel keen to put Belinda and Brenda's sleeping issues to bed

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel to Surrey to meet upcycler Rob, who has a range of sheds a few miles from Gatwick Airport

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Malindi the eastern black rhino is pregnant, but she's not her usual self. After giving birth unexpectedly during the middle of the day, the keepers hope that her lively baby, Jumaane, will bring her out of her shell and restore her independent spirit. Dusky pademelon males Kai and Aru have been fighting with each other, so the staff decide to split them up

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Angel transforms the attic into a studio and storage space for her vintage treasures and takes Dorothy on a trip to Paris, while Dick has his own plans for repurposing some ancient tools he finds in the outbuildings. The couple throws a surprise birthday celebration for Angel's mother, where Dick's purpose-built open-fire popcorn maker is put to the test

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits carpenter Bill Bradley and his wife Sarah, who set out to build two timber houses on a very limited space on the site of an old wood workshop in south London. The presenter finds out whether their dream of a mortgage-free life came true despite the complications posed by the awkward location, and a soaring budget

programme.title: Japan's Tsunami: How It Happened
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the scientific factors behind the magnitude nine earthquake and the resultant tsunami that struck Japan on March 11, 2011. Professor of geological sciences Roger Bilham views the effects of the devastation from the air, and journalist Callum Macrae travels to the north of the country, where survivors in the fishing villages of Sendai and Ofunato are struggling to cope in the aftermath

programme.title: The Tudors
programme.desc: In return for his denunciation of Martin Luther, the Pope christens Henry 'Defender of the Faith' but a brush with death makes the monarch keen to find a solution to his lack of an heir. Meanwhile, his desire for Anne Boleyn grows increasingly strong

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Staff at St George's Hospital tackle one of their most dramatic emergencies when 60-year-old rail worker Alan is airlifted to the helipad after being hit by a high-speed train. A CT scan reveals he has suffered multiple fractures and loss of blood flow to his right arm. Without circulation he will lose the limb, so the medical team tries everything to save it, including complicated surgery. Meanwhile, 95-year-old Betty is brought in after experiencing breathing difficulties at home, and retired engineer James, 63, has a nasty cut to his head and weakness in his arms following a tumble down the stairs

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the popular quiz as Sean Lock and David Mitchell compete against Jon Richardson and Katherine Ryan. The Brett Domino Trio join Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: With the hospital on lockdown after the Rikers patients escape, Sharpe and Max find themselves in a situation that could cost them their lives. Bloom must put aside her pain to help a patient, while Iggy is forced to face his marriage's issues. Medical drama, starring Ryan Eggold

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Staff at St George's Hospital tackle one of their most dramatic emergencies when 60-year-old rail worker Alan is airlifted to the helipad after being hit by a high-speed train. A CT scan reveals he has suffered multiple fractures and loss of blood flow to his right arm. Without circulation he will lose the limb, so the medical team tries everything to save it, including complicated surgery. Meanwhile, 95-year-old Betty is brought in after experiencing breathing difficulties at home, and retired engineer James, 63, has a nasty cut to his head and weakness in his arms following a tumble down the stairs

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team returns to explore the secrets behind mass-produced food, meeting technicians, scientists, factory owners, growers and producers, finding out exactly what goes into the everyday groceries that end up on the supermarket shelves. Jimmy Doherty finds out how Sri Lanka is coping with a looming coconut shortage, while Kate Quilton discovers whether compostables could be the answer to worldwide plastic pollution problem, annd Matt Tebbutt is in Paris to learn why the macaron has such a hefty price tag

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps Jannine and Shaun Cartwright build treasure chests for their young boys' toys with help from furniture-maker Anselm Fraser

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: After a road trip along the Spanish coastal region of Almeria, Frans and Julie Mampaeij from Berkshire completely fell in love with this part of the country. Now they want to seize the day and buy a holiday home here. With a cash budget of £90,000, Scarlette Douglas shows them five properties for them to choose between

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo head to Wales to see how can create the most profit on items, including an oil dispenser, fire grate, chaise longue and even a catamaran

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien attempt to restore items including an old cheese press, and a 1980s motorbike almost gets the better of Henry and his restorer Guy

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Angel transforms the attic into a studio and storage space for her vintage treasures and takes Dorothy on a trip to Paris, while Dick has his own plans for repurposing some ancient tools he finds in the outbuildings. The couple throws a surprise birthday celebration for Angel's mother, where Dick's purpose-built open-fire popcorn maker is put to the test

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Juana and Trevor Jones, who plan to knock down some 300-year-old decrepit barns, and build themselves a stylish retirement home in the Lincolnshire countryside. While sceptical about their ability to do this on a £100,000 budget, Charlie soon realises goung over-budget may be the least of the couple's problems. Ex-builder Trevor is determined to be hands-on with the project, and even refuses to down tools when he is facing a life-saving operation

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Inn at the Park in Aberdeen, where for owners Ronnie and Steph Caird, it's all about keeping it in the family. When the guests arrive, design-conscious father and son Steve and Allard take a trip back in time, and John isn't flushed with praise for his bathroom. At dinner, John reveals his culinary background, piling on the pressure for Ronnie to deliver a good breakfast. Fiona and Shauna's sizeable confession raises eyebrows, while in the morning, Ronnie has a breakfast to forget and the bathroom blues leave Ronnie and Steph seeking answers from their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Renfrewshire and Ingliston Country Club, where events manager Shaunna Kirk and sales manager Fiona Gallagher are a force to be reckoned with

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit, the group heads to 1692 Wasdale in the Lake District, where owners Steve and Allard Newell have proudly built their B&B from the ground up

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests stay at the Lynbar Hotel in Blackpool, where Ronnie and Steph have parking problems, while confident owner John O'Donnell boasts of serving the best breakfast in town

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to settle some scores. Inn at the Park owners Ronnie and Steph Caird are intent on clearing up John's issues with the toilet

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Wokingham in Berkshire to meet professional gardener Mark whose barns are brimful of bikes, bric-a-brac and baggage

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Giant otter couple Diego and Icana are the best of friends but have never mated, so keepers hope a new male will have what it takes to relight Icana's fire. Blue morpho butterflies Macuna and Peanut also need to mate, but when their diet of fruit ferments and turns to alcohol, their drunken behaviour might dash Chester's hopes of producing the next generation of butterflies. Cameras also follow the relationship between mum-and-daughter Andrean bears Lima and Madidi, who are fighting over food and personal space. Can the youngster learn to respect her elder?

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Angel redesigns the rooms in one of the chateau's turrets with a fruits and flowers motif - but an unexpected surprise lurks behind her creations. Outside, Dick is putting together a parkour fitness trail in the 12 acres of grounds, but hits upon an unexpected obstacle buried deep underground

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: Siobhán McSweeney hosts the semi-final of the pottery contest, in which the four remaining contestants face their biggest challenge to date. They must make a decorative chamber pot and a fully functional pedestal sink, before judges Keith Brymer Jones and Rich Miller decide which three potters will be going through to the final

programme.title: Deutschland 89
programme.desc: Various agencies vie for control of Martin, al of them offering him the same options - get arrested, get hired or get killed. However, he knows he must find a way to keep Max safe. Brigitte Winkelmann and CIA agent Hector Valdez task Martin with infiltrating a group of West Germans. Rose pays Lenora a visit, while Schweppenstette begrudgingly accepts a mission in Frankfurt. Drama, starring Jonas Nay, Raul Casso and Maria Schrader. In German

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Eleven-month-old Isabel is rushed to St George's after suffering a seizure and losing consciousness, while 22-year-old Felix is brought to A&E with a self-inflicted stab wound to his stomach. Retired engineer Bill, 68, fell 15ft from a ladder while cutting a leylandii hedge. He arrives by air ambulance with breathing difficulties and suspected multiple fractures

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Peter, 85, has been brought - unwillingly - to A&E by his stepson Brian. He cut his finger on some wire two weeks earlier and his hand is swollen and won't stop bleeding. Emma, 23, is rushed to St George's after crashing her car on the way to her father's house, while doctors also see to 86-year-old Harry, who fell down a flight of stairs a day earlier but didn't seek treatment. Ciaran, 27, injured his ankle the previous night while out with friends and was given a temporary cast. Emergency nurse practitioner Helen needs to get him X-rayed and replastered, but he faces a return visit for an operation

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Father Jack is hauled off to the local old priests' home to be treated for a strange medical complaint. He is replaced by a younger man - who proceeds to drive everyone crazy by playing jungle music at ear-splitting volume for hours on end. Dermot Morgan and Frank Kelly star

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The Craggy Island clerics face the prospect of an untimely death during the return flight from a visit to a holy shrine. Jack quickly commandeers the only parachute and bails out, taking the drinks trolley with him - leaving Ted and Dougal to their fate

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Sean Kelly and the bidders head to Poplar, East London, where Heavy D goes through his blue period after driving the other bidders up the wall with his antics

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May take a look at increasingly popular tall hatchbacks known as crossovers. Having concluded their primary appeal is to caravanners, the duo spend a couple of days in models by Mazda and Volkswagen and try to get a feel for what life is like for the people they believe would buy one. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond drives a pair of exciting Lamborghinis in Italy - the ferocious Aventador Roadster and the limited-edition Sesto Elemento, which carries an eye-watering price tag of around £2million. Two cars are also put through their paces on the test track, with James in a modern re-creation of the classic Porsche 911 and Aerosmith frontman Steven Tyler in the rather less exotic Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson drives the Jaguar F-Type sports car on some of Britain's finest roads, while Richard Hammond jumps inside the Range Rover Sport and gives it a thorough workout off-road, on a track and in what he believes is its most natural habitat - the lanes of Cheshire. James May goes for a countryside adventure in the New Routemaster London bus brought into use in 2013, which is propelled by a turbodiesel generator and a powerful electric motor. Inspired by the machines they have tested, the trio then meet to celebrate Britain's motor industry with a grand finale in front of Buckingham Palace. Plus, former Formula One driver Mark Webber takes to the track for a lap in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's India
programme.desc: The chef begins an exploration of the country's food in the states of West Bengal and Tamil Nadu, where the influence of the British Empire on eating habits can still be observed. Rick enjoys the prominence of fish and seafood in the cuisine found in the cities of Kolkata and Chennai, cooking dishes including prawns in coconut milk and his mum's British Raj curry

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are in Russia as the motoring magazine returns, taking part in a race across the urban landscape of St Petersburg. James defends the honour of the car in a very small Renault, Richard tries to prove that pedalling is faster by riding a bicycle, while Jeremy attempts to demonstrate that water is the quickest way across town by piloting a hovercraft. To make matters more complicated, all three presenters are racing not only one another but also the Stig, who is making his way toward the finish line by public transport. Plus, the new Lamborghini Huracan is put through its paces and singer-songwriter Ed Sheeran takes to the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters embark on a road trip across Australia's Northern Territory in fast, luxurious GT cars, with Jeremy Clarkson choosing a BMW M6 Gran Coupe, Richard Hammond picking a Bentley Continental GT V8 and James May opting for a Nissan GT-R. The trio set out on an adventure that takes in crocodile-infested rivers, a race around a vast opencast mine and an encounter with one of the world's longest lorries, concluding with an unusual challenge involving a farm, their cars and 4,000 cows. Plus, 24 star Kiefer Sutherland takes to the track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The competitors are lured to a wasteland for a challenge involving eggs and medical equipment. David Baddiel spray-paints his face and Jo Brand reveals her psychic superpowers. Presented by Greg Davies, with help from co-host Alex Horne

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? USA
programme.desc: Actress Lea Thompson guests on the classic improv show with Wayne Brady, Jonathan Mangum, Colin Mochrie, Ryan Stiles and hosted by Aisha Tyler

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? USA
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler welcomes Katie Cassidy to the classic improv show, with regulars Wayne Brady, Brad Sherwood, Colin Mochrie and Ryan Stiles.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: David Mitchell is joined by MasterChef judge Gregg Wallace and actor Nigel Havers, while Lee Mack teams up with writer and comedian Charlie Brooker and actress Nina Wadia, as the comedy panel show hosted by Rob Brydon continues

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Host Stephen Fry welcomes Jack Dee, Chris Addison, Rich Hall and regular panellist Alan Davies to the quiz with a difference. He asks them a range of questions on the subject of illumination, and awards points for the most interesting answers

programme.title: QI

programme.title: Rick Stein's India
programme.desc: The chef visits Mumbai in search of the bombil, the fish used in Bombay duck, and also enjoys Parsee hospitality and learns about their contribution to Indian cuisine. He then travels south to Pondicherry, where locals prepare Indian dishes with a French twist, before heading north along the coast to Mamallapuram to sample one of the best fish curries he has ever had. Rick also demonstrates how to make popular Parsee chicken dish sali murgh and personal favourite aloo mattar

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May attempt to improve the ambulance, with the presenters all going their own separate ways to enhance one area they think could do with an overhaul. James focuses on passenger comfort, Richard tries to shorten response times by getting other drivers out of the way more effectively, an issue Jeremy tackles with the invention of what he calls the 'sports ambulance'. Their three very different creations are then put to the test with a series of severe and often messy challenges. Plus, Formula One driver Daniel Ricciardo is the Star in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh heads to more countries that are rarely visited by tourists to get a sense of the local culture and find out if they deserve their reputation. In this first edition, he heads to Zimbabwe in the aftermath of Robert Mugabe's rule, to discover whether British people should be holidaying in the southern African country once again. He meets Chipo, a teacher who was born and raised in Harare under Mugabe's rule. She explains that although people have been coming to the country, few venture beyond Victoria Falls. She wants to show Rom what they are missing on a grand, epic road trip that takes in National Parks and close-up encounters with rhinos, elephants and the so-called 'cheetah ambassador'

programme.title: Meet the Richardsons

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey through southern Scotland, with this leg beginning in the industrial town of Greenock, from where he sets sail in the world's last sea-going paddle steamer. He then sees the devastating effects of a fire at the Glasgow School of Art, heads to Blantyre to find out about the humble beginnings of a famous missionary and explorer and learns to make sourdough in a bakery in Strathaven before finishing his trip in Larkhall

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Motherwell as he continues his journey through southern Scotland, celebrating the Victorian iron and steel industry and admires one of its crowning achievements - the Forth Rail Bridge. He then visits the Falls of Clyde, which inspired Wordsworth and Coleridge, heads to Cumbernauld to learn about the origins of one of Scotland's best-selling soft drinks, and in Linlithgow he marvels at the ingenuity of the engineers who built the Union Canal

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Kelvingrove Art Gallery and Museum. Finds brought in to be valued include some jewellery from the art deco era, a painting by artistic group the Glasgow Girls, and an ugly family heirloom that turns out to have been made by a very important historical figure - and is worth far more than its owners suspected

programme.title: Impossible Railways

programme.title: Train Truckers

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A juggernaut of Soviet engineering which was crucial to the international space race. Deserted Scottish coastal defences and a complex that's crucial to American history

programme.title: World War Weird

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

In Perth, he finds out how to make a sporran and catches the Highland Main Line to Pitlochry and one of Queen Victoria's favourite haunts, then finishes with a dram in Scotland's smallest distillery

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.desc: Caroline Wardrope hosts the final dinner party in Glasgow, and throws a 1970s-themed disco evening with a mystery menu. programme.desc: The chef travels to Philadelphia to help failing Mexican restaurant Zocalo. Now Gordon tries to turn things around before the floundering business ruins their marriage and tears their family apart

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman shows five properties to recently retired West Sussex couple Alison and Martin, who are looking to buy a holiday home along Spain's Costa Dorada

There is also a visit to the Sussex countryside and a tree-house that hovers among the trees

programme.title: Grayson's Art Club

Grayson and Russell also choose their favourites from the public submissions to this episode. The Turner Prize-winner also talks to celebrated land artist Andy Goldsworthy, who's been creating art during lockdown on his farm in Scotland, and to Polly Morgan, who is renowned for her artworks that feature taxidermy

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: The charity version of the popular baking show returns, and with Noel Fielding on paternity leave, Matt Lucas is presenting on his own. To impress Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith, the celebrities serve up millionaire shortbread, a fruity tart and their pet hates in cake form in the showstopper

Chloe from Cardiff wants advice on 'pegging', and personal trainer Tracy is concerned that she might be addicted to sex. Finally, barman Tom needs an STI test and Jordan is worried about a lump on his bum

As always, Jimmy is helped by a whole host of celebrity question-setters, including newsreader Jon Snow and actor Charles Dance

programme.title: Stand Up & Deliver
programme.desc: Part two of two. With just 24 hours to go until their comedy debut, the celebrities get some last-minute tips from their mentors. Judi Love coaches Curtis Pritchard in improvisation, Nick Helm teaches Sayeeda Warsi how to make a strong first impression, and Jason Manford is still struggling to get Shaun Ryder to remember his act. Meanwhile, Katie McGlynn tries to harness her nerves and Richard Coles remains reverent when mentor David Baddiel pushes on him a joke with two swear words in the same sentence

programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at two bungalows requiring different solutions. In Purley, Croydon, Christine Attwood and Tony Congdon have been going back and forth for almost a decade about the future of their unusual bungalow. In Larne in Northern Ireland, Catherine and Nigel Finch have hit a spot of turbulence due to their dated, 1970s-style home. Nigel enjoys the rural location's incredible views, but Catherine has never loved the place and misses having neighbours, so Phil searches for a home that pleases them both

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: The charity version of the popular baking show returns, and with Noel Fielding on paternity leave, Matt Lucas is presenting on his own. In previous editions, comedian Tom Allen has stepped in to co-present - but today he is showing off his baking skills as one of the contestants, alongside Star Wars actress Daisy Ridley, comedian Rob Beckett and singer Alexandra Burke. To impress Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith, the celebrities serve up millionaire shortbread, a fruity tart and their pet hates in cake form in the showstopper

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets architect and shed fanatic Micah Jones, who plans to transform an old agricultural building in Co Down. Micah plans an upside-down, four-bedroom shed using new techniques and materials, but the £200,000 budget runs short and Micah is forced to take on some of the building work himself

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary follows the work of the stroke team at south London's King's College Hospital. Laura's family faces a big decision when the 72-year-old grandmother is found to have a blood clot on her brain.The medics also treat 83-year-old Marjorie, who is complaining of a pain behind her eye following a recent bout of shingles, and 84-year-old Ronald, who has been bitten by his own dog

programme.title: The Sex Clinic
programme.desc: Student Taps comes to the clinic as he has never been able to ejaculate during sex, and he's hoping that the resident therapist will help him overcome his problem. Chloe from Cardiff wants advice on 'pegging', and personal trainer Tracy is concerned that she might be addicted to sex. Finally, barman Tom needs an STI test and Jordan is worried about a lump on his bum

programme.title: Our Baby: A Modern Miracle
programme.desc: Documentary following transgender couple Hannah and Jake Graf as they start a family. Filmed over the course of a year, the programme follows Hannah and Jake from their first meeting with a surrogate through to their baby's birth in the midst of lockdown. The film also explores their experiences of transitioning and the prejudice they've faced, as they strive to realise their dream of becoming parents

programme.title: The Undateables
programme.desc: Emily, who has a learning disability, is determined to find her first proper boyfriend, but she has to overcome her nerves and her tendency to talk too much. Meanwhile, Poetry lover Michael, who has Asperger's, has been single for 14 years and hopes he can one day find love, and dance teacher Tobi is fearless on the dance floor, but is something of a novice when it comes to romance

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Documentary following the staff of Wood Green animal charity, who are committed to matching their homeless dogs with prospective owners. An eight-stone Newfoundland makes a favourable first impression on the Coxon family, but staff are worried that his habit of showering everyone with a spray of slobber may take some of the gloss off. Nine-week-old French bulldog Kevin competes with a nervous terrier to win the hearts of loved-up couple Ryan and Katie, while a recently widowed grandmother seeks another 'beating heart' to help fill a home that is feeling very empty

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Persian cat that has been hit by a car, requiring treatments involving a skin graft and putting its crushed bones back together. Meanwhile, a rescue dog has a cancerous tumour in his jaw removed, and a young staffie-cross needs treatment for a developmental elbow disease

programme.title: Drawers Off
programme.desc: Graphic designer Alpha takes his turn to model for the group. Abstract artist Jilly sees something in Alpha's choice of pose that nobody else does. Jon tries out a different drawing style, while Steph experiments with an unusual tool to add texture to her work. Presented by Jenny Eclair

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Cameras return to France, three years on, to catch up with two campsite owners whose strict rules on-site have apparently put their business at risk. After facing a major setback in their first year, have a pair of hoteliers managed to stay the course? Plus, a revisit to an enterprising couple in Spain as their handmade soap and candle company takes a new direction to survive

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Photographer Nat and budding artist and writer Ruth from Leicester fell in love with Tarn in France on holiday and were so inspired by the region's quaint villages and beautiful scenery that they now want to buy their very own holiday home there. Laura Hamilton lines up five properties for their budget of £90,000, but will any of them be the perfect place?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Peter and Julia Andrews from Hull love Fuerteventura in the Canary Islands and want to buy a holiday home there, which their whole family can enjoy. Armed with a budget of £60,000, Sara Damergi scours the island for five perfect lock-up-and-leave properties, but will any of them make the grade?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Gary and Carol from Kent seek a home in Aquitaine, France, so house-hunting expert Laura Hamilton tries to find them the perfect property

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Make-up artist and female impersonator Marcus Hunter-Neill hopes to impress with a red carpet-themed evening as he hosts the first party in Belfast. However, his alter ego Lady Portia Diamante steals the limelight as he competes for the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Jamaican lifeguard Lenny Irving serves up Caribbean cuisine at the second of the week's dinner parties in Belfast. He also hopes to win over his guests by complementing his menu with strong rum - and seems particularly keen to impress Merchant Navy officer Fiona Rush

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Freelance feminist writer Cat Anderson hosts the third dinner party in Belfast, and hopes to win over her guests with her bohemian chic style and live music. However, her efforts are wasted on lifeguard Lenny Irving, who is only interested in Fiona Rush

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth dinner-party challenge from Belfast is hosted by charity worker and confident cook Rosie Smyth. She decides the best way to win over her guests is to have a 'come-as-your-teenage-self' evening, and a zingy mojito cheesecake

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Merchant Navy officer Fiona Rush hosts the final dinner party in Belfast, and hopes to impress with a nautical-themed evening. However, one of her guests is unimpressed with the amount of fish on the menu, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Charlie's Italian bistro in La Verne, southern California, where the owner's lack of business nous is putting her relatives' homes at risk, after her mother and sister put them up as collateral. To make matters worse, the restaurant's food is less than appetising and its long-serving chef seems to be incompetent

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Paul and Debbie from Staffordshire would love to find a holiday home on the beautiful island of Fuerteventura where they can enjoy spending quality time together. They're looking for a two-bedroom apartment near the attractive coastal town of Caleta de Fuste and have a budget of £115,000. Ben Hillman shows them five properties from them to choose between

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Gary and Carol from Kent seek a home in Aquitaine, France, so house-hunting expert Laura Hamilton tries to find them the perfect property

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Cornish adaptive surfer Pegleg plans to extend his VW campervan by creating a new cabin from scratch, while Martin and Carol-Anne want to build a glamping pod in their Pembrokeshire garden, using a dome that originally housed an electricity generator at the London Olympics. George also visits an earthquake-resistant glass house on the coast of western Japan, and looks at aluminium panels as a new kind of canopy for his treehouse

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: The host visits Ben at his ground floor apartment in St Leonards-on-Sea, East Sussex. George's challenge is to turn a grand drawing room with sea views into a cool, elegant kitchen-diner and create rich, opulent schemes for a bedroom and study. In Manchester, he tackles a 1930s semi-detached where the compact dimensions are proving a headache. With only a tiny budget to work with, can he make a cramped galley kitchen both functional and beautiful?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary follows the work of the stroke team at south London's King's College Hospital. Laura's family faces a big decision when the 72-year-old grandmother is found to have a blood clot on her brain.The medics also treat 83-year-old Marjorie, who is complaining of a pain behind her eye following a recent bout of shingles, and 84-year-old Ronald, who has been bitten by his own dog

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A 23-year-old virgin from Kent picks a partner from six nude candidates - but this proves a nerve-racking experience, since he has never seen a naked woman in his life. Meanwhile, a 27-year-old beauty therapist from London is determined to have a baby before she turns 30, and hopes that one of the men lurking behind the screens will be the perfect father

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, be they entertainment juggernauts, hard-hitting documentaries, news reports or gritty dramas

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, focusing on The Late Late Show with James Corden and Prince Harry, Married at First Sight Australia, Drawers Off, Million Pound Pawn, Your Honor, Pooch Perfect, Stand Up and Deliver, and ITV news of Donald Trump's speech

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: The charity version of the popular baking show returns, and with Noel Fielding on paternity leave, Matt Lucas is presenting on his own. programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: Siobhán McSweeney hosts the semi-final of the pottery contest, in which the four remaining contestants face their biggest challenge to date. They must make a decorative chamber pot and a fully functional pedestal sink, before judges Keith Brymer Jones and Rich Miller decide which three potters will be going through to the final

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Documentary following the staff of Wood Green animal charity, who are committed to matching their homeless dogs with prospective owners. An eight-stone Newfoundland makes a favourable first impression on the Coxon family, but staff are worried that his habit of showering everyone with a spray of slobber may take some of the gloss off. programme.desc: Eight more privileged youngsters spend a week on the breadline, including Maria - daughter of Russian multi-millionaires - who meets Becky and Mohammed in Croydon

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: A dodgy scrap dealer is spotted up to no good in County Durham, but his attempt to flee the grime scene is captured in evidentially invaluable technicolour. In Surrey, a pair of water engineers head out to tackle the damage caused to the UK's sewer system by some of the 11 billion litres of effluent flushed down British U-bends every day, while Malvern's enforcement officers Dan and Jude spend their days hunting for fly-tippers and rubbish dumpers, but need to get their prevention message across in a different way

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The owner of a tanning salon regrets letting an employee move into a house he owns when he is faced with complaints from the neighbours about wild parties and £3,000 in unpaid rent. A couple managing a property for their daughter arrange to sell it, only to find the tenant refuses to leave, while a housing enforcement officer goes in search of a landlord who has left a tenant without light, heat or running water

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Teorin and Collette had high hopes when they let out their five-bedroom family home, but it wasn't long before their tenant stopped paying rent - and then turned the house into a hostel for holiday-makers. Quickly running out of options, the couple turn to eviction specialist Chris Sharpe for help. Plus, Harrow council officers discover 40 people crammed into a potential deathtrap of a house, and a woman who relies on her rent for medical expenses tries to claw back some from her tenant

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Elmor are in Manchester to recover £4,000 owed to a firm of architects, only for the debtor to attempt to throw them off the scent. Stewart is joined by Vic in Liverpool to track down a landlord who owes his former tenants deposits totalling £5,000, while Gareth and Mitch are in for a surprise when they chase a builder in Wiltshire

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team have their hands full when a raid reveals a crowbar-wielding suspect with a house full of drugs, a Taser and an offensive mouth

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jon puts his body on the line as he tackles a drunken youth in a domestic dispute and Martin turns detective to catch a drink-driver who thinks she can get one over on the interceptors. A pizza delivery driver take fast food to the extreme, Doug gets to grips with a burglar and the hunt is on for Lincoln's answer to Harry Houdini

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Sgt Carlin arrests a gang of van thieves and uncovers a major criminal operation in the process, while a late-night compost thief is caught on his way out of the shower. Officers hunt for a trucker wielding a baseball bat who has apparently succumbed to road rage and vegetarian Craggy rides to the rescue of a lorry load of calves on the A46. Elsewhere, Brian cracks down on a barbaric blood sport, Jon faces an unexploded bomb, and two fuel thieves plough straight into the interceptors' precious BMW

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul, Steve and Ben make a shocking discovery when they break into a home in Essex while serving an eviction notice on a single mother and her two children. Meanwhile, Stewart and Iain receive more than just threats from the staff and owner of a taxi business, a car dealer has to choose between paying up or losing his showroom stars, and an Indian restaurant owner is in danger of losing his livelihood

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul Bohill and Steve and Ben Pinner face their biggest eviction yet - taking on squatters in a large central London office block. With the help of the police, they try to remove a suspected group of 70 from the building, but with a barricade in place, the team faces a challenging operation ahead. Elsewhere, officers are confronted by an army of family and friends when they attempt to enforce a writ at the property of a debtor, and face a difficult eviction at the north London home of a heavily pregnant tenant and her family, after the council fails to pay the rent to the landlord

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Dipi is forced to face the consequences of her actions. Hendrix, unable to take a hint, arranges another horribly misguided romantic gesture towards Harlow

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Jasmine distracts herself with exercise, while Justin and Leah realise they have only one day to prepare an engagement party for Christian and Tori

programme.title: Britain's Parking Hell
programme.desc: Cameras follow PC Mark 'Robocop' Hodson, a policeman on a mission to restore parking law and order to the streets of Birmingham. He sets his sights on the city centre, where one unsuspecting pavement parker is about to feel the force of his zero-tolerance approach to bad parking. In Manchester, a band of baby-faced enforcers are turning the tables on the parents who break the parking rules at the school gates, and driving instructor extraordinaire Andy is hoping to instil some law and order into panic-parker and panto performer Liam

programme.title: Inside McDonalds: The 300 Billion Dollar Burger
programme.desc: The history of the fast food chain, examining its origins and its spread to become an international company, with outlets opening in Britain in the 1970s and even Russia embracing the franchise in 1990. The film also examines the controversies that beset the company in the 1990s, with scandals concerning unhealthy eating, advertising to children and animal welfare

programme.title: The Green Mile (1999)
programme.desc: A convicted child-killer of towering height and massive build is condemned to death row, but astonishes the guards with his good nature and miraculous healing powers. They believe the gentle giant is incapable of crime, and emotions run high as they realise there is no hope of overturning his death sentence. Supernatural drama, based on a novel by Stephen King, starring Tom Hanks, Michael Clarke Duncan, David Morse and Harry Dean Stanton

programme.title: Countdown to Murder
programme.desc: Documentary examining some of Britain's most notorious murders, charting the last few days leading up to the killing from the perspectives of both perpetrator and victim. The programme uses dramatic reconstruction to pinpoint the trigger that set each one in motion, providing in-depth psychological analysis and scrutiny of the devious and calculating plans the killers formulated

programme.title: Born to Kill?
programme.desc: Canadian couple Paul Bernardo and Karla Homolka appeared to epitomise love's young dream when they were married, but they were serial killers who raped and murdered three teenage girls - one of them Homolka's 15-year-old sister Tammy. This film delves into their backgrounds and talks to those who knew them in an effort to find out what turned them into killers, while detectives, journalists and criminal psychologists also offer an insight into their motives

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Internet entrepreneur Martin and his partner in grime Glenn hear about a huge fly-tipping operation blighting a remote Hampshire beauty spot, and raise a small army of volunteers who roll up their sleeves to sort it themselves. Meanwhile, the UK's network of motorways and A-roads rely upon a system of lay-bys to help keep drivers safe, but these safe pull-in zones also attract an unsightly collection of filthy behaviour, and it falls to Huntingdon lay-by warriors Clive and Steve to take on the speeding traffic, atrocious weather, drunk drivers - and those people who confuse lay-bys with lavatories

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: An east London landlord who trusted his property to a letting agency discovers the house is being advertised on a Romanian website - and strangers have moved in. A South Yorkshire single mum is owed £10,000 in back rent when her tenant disappears, and has to prove he has abandoned the property to get it back. Plus, west London housing officers investigate a tip-off about people living in dangerous conditions behind a row of shops

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Susan and Gary bought an East End flat to let out and were approached by a letting agent who promised hassle-free guaranteed rent. But before they knew it, he had broken in and changed the locks. Now they are fighting to get the property back. Meanwhile, landlord Paul discovers that his seriously ill father's long-standing tenant owes a staggering £20,000 in rent for living in her Notting Hill home

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Matt and Garry try to recover more than £6,000 owed in nursery fees in Warwickshire, while Gary and Cona head to West Sussex, chasing £11,000 owed by a businessman who has defaulted on a previous plan, and have no option but to break into the property. The pair also head down to the south coast of England, seeking more than £3,500 owed for an unpaid loan

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers from Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit are at the forefront of a raid against suspected drug traffickers, the hunt is on for a driver who has fled the scene of a horrific crash, and Rich Precious and Sean Baxter are left lagging far behind as they chase a speeding biker. Dan Brigginshaw is on duty at Spalding's annual flower show, and Sean Brennan deals with a young man who thinks he can throw his weight around after a boozy night out

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A van thief gives officers from Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit the runaround before taking a disastrous wrong turn. Dan joins a manhunt but finds dodgy diesel instead, while Jon wades into a late-night assault involving a pair of TV legends. Big Jim reports a boy racer to his mum and delivers a dressing down to a driver with a taste for rude gestures

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dog handler Jon is forced to wrestle with a drunk man on the high street in Lincoln and Dan has an early morning encounter with a driver who is over the limit. Elsewhere, a wine-loving shoplifter gets his just desserts, Mark pulls over a juggernaut in jeopardy on the A1, and the helicopter is scrambled to chase down a car thief

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul and Steve make an extraordinary discovery inside a locked bedroom, and immediately call for police back-up. Brian and Graham head to Swindon Town football club to recover a debt, while Paul and Ben have difficulty evicting a couple from a house in Kent after learning they have six children and nowhere else to go

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Documentary giving an insight into cash-strapped Britain from the perspectives of debtors, creditors and those who collect the payments, including High Court enforcement bailiffs and repossession teams. Paul and Ben are called in to evict squatters from a residential property, but something about the squat does not quite add up for the team

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Harlow tells Hendrix about Brent's upbringing in an attempt to stop the hostilities between the two. It remains to be seen whether Shane will move onwards with Amy

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Ziggy and Tane are caught off guard when Mac arrives home early, and Susie showers John with encouragement to run for Surf Club president

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: The opening night of Dave and Tony's Mexican restaurant looks like being a washout when the rain pours down - and the weather doesn't exactly help mother-and-daughter bar owners Lisa and Tia when they advertise a Friday night barbecue. Dog rescuer Linda is worried about sick pup Donny, while Sheffield retirees Phil and Jackie - known for their colourful, matching outfits - scour the charity shops for more crazy clothing bargains

programme.title: Casualty 24/7
programme.desc: This time, paediatric sister Jemma Wray treats two critically ill children - 10-year-old Kai has been having seizures, and 18-month-old Joel is struggling to breathe. Elsewhere, nurse Will Gibbons looks after a 28-year-old patient who has been in a motorbike accident, and 51-year-old Andrew comes into the hospital after damaging his fingers with a circular saw

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Critical care paramedic Rob and doctor Adam fight to save the life of a boy who has been stabbed in the street. The 16-year-old has been knifed in the neck, has lost a significant amount of blood and is struggling to breathe. Elsewhere, Tom Waters is dispatched to 30-year-old bricklayer Daniel, who has sliced his arm with a power saw. He was cutting timber on a workbench using an angle grinder with a circular blade when he lost control and it sliced through his right hand

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A patient brought into Hull Royal Infirmary by police claims to have swallowed drugs, but he refuses to co-operate with staff, putting them under further pressure. In resus, a patient's life hangs in the balance as the team assess the extent of a serious brain bleed, which could be life-altering, while a 17-year-old arrives after injuring his ankle at a skate park

programme.title: Extraordinary People
programme.desc: The story of 16-year-old Mithun Chauhan, who suffers from an extremely rare genetic condition that has left him with large swollen lumps all over his face and body. His disfigurement has led to the creation of a rumour that his village has become home to a reclusive monster. The condition, known as neurofibroma, affects one in 33,000 people and causes tumours to grow along the nerve endings, with Mithun's case among the most extreme ever discovered

programme.title: Plastic Surgery Knifemares
programme.desc: A woman whose cut-price tummy tuck in Turkey almost killed her, and a man's quest to become a Korean pop star that led him to spend £100,000 to look like his idols

programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: Behind the scenes insight at a specialist clinic where four top dermatologists - Dr Natalia Spierings, Dr Adil Sheraz, Dr Dev Shah and Dr Emma McMullen - treat patients with a range of skin conditions and concerns, including acne, psoriasis and cysts

Now they need to get her out or they could lose the house. Elsewhere, a tenant uses scare tactics to keep his landlord off his back, and in Harrow, council housing officers investigate a residential property that has been turned into a brothel

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The tale of an elderly couple whose hopes of a peaceful retirement were ruined when their tenants stopped paying rent, then moved in their abusive friends who became squatters. With the house becoming a free-for-all and in a terrible state of repair, and the couple facing a barrage of verbal threats and abusive behaviour, they have been forced to seek help in an attempt to get their property back. Plus, a man who cannot sell his house because his sitting tenant refuses to leave

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Gary and Paul visit Kent to recover £2,500 owed by a publican to a utilities supplier - only to find themselves in the middle of a family at war. Stewart and Elmor deal with a sensitive case in Liverpool, as they repossess a property where the tenant has failed to pay any rent for almost a year

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A violent man gives officers the runaround after wreaking havoc in a hospital, a Macedonian truck driver has a bad day and a fuel thief is caught red-handed. Meanwhile, two Audi S3s are put through their paces by the team and the Operational Support Unit goes back to the classroom for a course about dealing with riots

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Sergeant Baxter strikes it lucky when a routine check on a car uncovers a case of suspected drug dealing, and things turn nasty when three men are arrested on suspicion of theft. Elsewhere, Jon Peacock brings in two prisoners, a petrol thief's stealing spree comes to a halt after a high-speed pursuit, and Jules and Jezz come to the aid of an abandoned dog in a ditch

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The Operational Support Unit has to evade a guard dog when carrying out a drugs raid on a farm, while a driver thinks he can pull a fast one by impersonating his brother. Barry finds himself dealing with an irate nightclubber, Craggy has an early morning encounter with a polecat and it is a case of deja vu for Dan as he deals with the same person twice in one shift

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Brian O'Shaughnessy and Graham Aldred's first case sees them in a Brighton health spa as they search for assets to seize in pursuit of a £20,000 debt. Following a stand-off, they devise a way to work with the owner and her professional advisers to come up with a solution. Later, Graham reveals his softer side when he revisits a woman who defaulted on a debt payment plan

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul and Steve's first job is an eviction with a twist when they find out the landlord is the tenant's son, while their next case is also tricky and involves family members with a question mark over their immigration status who are now facing homelessness. Their final case involves a man with a history of mental illness who has reached the end of his tenancy but refuses to leave, and Steve tries to find a solution after a church minister and his mother fail to placate him

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Hendrix comes to terms with the damage he has caused Brent. Chloe returns early, having decided not to help Fay. Toadie invites Melanie to dinner

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Dean is back on the bottle, furious after discovering Mac knew about Ziggy and Tane. Justin's secret planning has caused everyone to think he is sick again

programme.title: Cats and Dogs at War
programme.desc: Chirag and Nanci break up the fights between a cat and a puppy belonging to a Devon couple, and try to control a dog in the Peak District that chases everything

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Meeting bargain and bartering supremo Tony Carey and his wife Claire, who moved onto the Saydo Park community less than a year ago and bought their mobile home for an absolute steal. There is also a visit to familiar faces Joe and Jan, who have been married for 47 years, and handed over their animal charity last year to their friend Jacqui, in a bid to try and enjoy their retirement. Then the unthinkable happened, when 69-year-Jan was diagnosed with breast cancer

programme.title: The Rainmaker (1997)
programme.desc: An idealistic newly qualified lawyer teams up with a disreputable researcher to champion the cause of a leukaemia sufferer whose mother is suing an insurance company for refusing to pay his medical costs. As the case progresses, the young legal eagle finds he has much to learn about the dark side of the judicial process. Francis Ford Coppola's courtroom drama adapted from the novel by John Grisham, starring Matt Damon, Danny DeVito, Jon Voight, Claire Danes and Mickey Rourke

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The family of a child who finds a gun suspected of being a murder weapon at a naval base comes under suspicion. The team sets out to discover who last used the firearm, with likely candidates including a petty criminal, a drug user, a Navy commander and a boy. Crime drama, starring Pauley Perrette and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: CIA agent Trent Kort returns to track down dangerous criminal Jonathan Siravo and Gibbs is forced to put his personal feelings aside to assist in the investigation. The duo manage to get hold of Siravo's accountant, who agrees to reveal all he knows in exchange for full immunity, but the trail leads to an old man in a coma in what seems to be a case of stolen identity

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A man is found murdered in a local park and the death is initially linked to drugs, but as Bernard and Lupo investigate they discover fingerprints at the scene that lead them to a man with learning difficulties who holds the key to the killing

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard try to solve the murder of a 17-year-old youth, and discover his younger brother had recently been banished from a polygamous Mormon sect. As they investigate the group's controversial leader, the detectives learn he had been trying to track down one of his wives. Crime drama, starring Jeremy Sisto, Anthony Anderson and Sam Waterston, with Colm Meaney (Star Trek: The Next Generation)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A wealthy Wall Street stockbroker's death looks like suicide, but Logan suspects otherwise and pins the blame on the man's protege. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the death of a troubled NYPD officer, a man with mounting financial difficulties who was killed by colleagues after taking hostages at gunpoint. As the pair dig deeper into his life, they discover a stash of valuable documents in his apartment as well as a body, and the evidence leads to a man claiming to be the illegitimate son of a famous president

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An investigation is launched when a war widow with a possible grudge against the army is found unconscious in the rubble of a bombed recruitment centre alongside her lifeless child. Lupo and Bernard begin their search for the bomber and encounter a number of suspects, including a man whose sister was killed in Iraq and the leader of a group of war protesters

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An old acquaintance of Logan's is found dead, and is presumed to have committed suidice until the investigation reveals he was recently in contact with a former priest accused of sex crimes

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An army scientist goes missing, so Abby is asked to temporarily replace him on a top-secret research project - but Gibbs later learns her assignment is connected to a current murder investigation, and worries she may meet the same fate as her predecessor. Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of a marine is found strung up at a construction site, the agents travel to LA to work with another NCIS team on an investigation into an international arms deal that escalates into a high-priority case, and Tony begins to pry into Ziva's life. Guest starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J (NCIS: Los Angeles)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Agent Callen poses as an arms trade contact in a bid to catch killer Michael Aaron Rivkin. Meanwhile, Tony fears Ziva may not be loyal to the team, and a psychologist uncovers the truth about the relationship between Gibbs and Agent Macy. With Chris O'Donnell, Louise Lombard and LL Cool J

programme.title: L.A.'s Finest
programme.desc: The team tracks down a budding serial killer who is luring young women into isolated areas before murdering them live on social media. Meanwhile, Syd's dangerous secret collides with McKenna's complicated past

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A woman is drugged and raped while working behind the bar at a party for a billionaire businessman

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A woman makes a false accusation, leaving her son faced with a deadly choice. Drama, starring Mariska Hargitay and Ice-T

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: As the investigation of Gary Munson goes on, his union takes action and threatens the lives in the SVU

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A young boy is found wandering alone in Central Park and the search for his parents leads to startling evidence of a planned mass casualty event in the city. As the SVU works with the joint-terrorism task force to find the suspects and the motive, Benson has to convince Barba to address allegations of rape and torture the boy's mother discloses

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Renard is rushed to hospital after being shot. Nick is forced to rely on Trubel to help him track down a new creature after losing his powers

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Renard's life is saved by a mysterious woman, Monroe and Rosalee search for a way to restore Nick's powers, and Trubel spies on the creature behind the recent murders

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when a man is crushed to death under a crane. Initially they suspect the construction company of foul play, but the case leads them to the victim's hospitalised wife, as well as plans for a controversial medical procedure

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the murder of a man visiting New York City to confront his past, but as they delve into the victim's personal life, the case becomes complicated when all clues point to a revered sheriff. Meanwhile, McCoy faces competition when a new district attorney is nominated for election

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: After a woman is killed during an attempt to shoot her husband, the team discover the couple were attending an infertility clinic where the doctor was involved in illegal practices. However, they find themselves unable to arrest him due to confidentiality rules, and his patients' reluctance to talk

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when a man is crushed to death under a crane. Initially they suspect the construction company of foul play, but the case leads them to the victim's hospitalised wife, as well as plans for a controversial medical procedure

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Bernard and Lupo investigate the suspected hit-and-run death of the mistress of the CEO of a failed investment bank. The evidence leads the pair to a kidnapping and extortion plot that uncovers several potential suspects, including a disgruntled client

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A taxi-driver is found dead in his car, and Logan follows a chain of evidence involving a loan shark, a forged cheque and a missing plumber that leads him to believe the victim's wife is the killer. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is forced to work alongside the FBI to catch the killer of an Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent who was murdered during a poker game at the Secretary of the Navy's mansion. Elsewhere, Tony takes on Rivkin in a confrontation that ends in one of them being shot

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team flies to Israel, but receives a hostile reception from Ziva's father, who is the head of intelligence agency Mossad. As events unfold, Gibbs learns that Ziva was romantically involved with Rivkin and finds himself forced to make a tough decision. Guest starring Michael Nouri, alongside Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony travels to North Africa on the trail of missing agent Ziva, who is thought to have been tracking down a terrorist leader called Saleem Ulman. Intent on exacting revenge for her apparent death during a flight to Somalia, he is taken hostage by militants and discovers she is still alive - only to be given a potentially catastrophic ultimatum. Starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson suspects Rob Miller is behind the mysterious death of a teenage girl found dead in the Hudson River, but cannot prove it. Despite having only circumstantial evidence, ADA Stone takes the case to court before Miller can destroy more lives. Mariska Hargitay stars

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team investigates a youth hockey team, where bullying of new players has taken a dangerous turn

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Fin revisits his military roots when a fellow Army ranger struggles to recover from a sexual assault. Sarah Booth guest stars, with Ice T and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A man convicted of rape is exonerated and released 16 years later. However, on the night of his release, the daughter of the woman he was convicted of raping is also raped and murdered - and he is SVU's prime suspect. Detective drama, starring Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A man uses a fake identity to lure a woman into bed, destroying her family, so Barba tries to get him convicted of rape - only to face a sceptical legal team. Raul Esparza stars

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: The Grimms go looking for Wesen at a local boxing club after the death of an ex-fighter. Chavez tries to recruit Trubel to a secret society, and Elizabeth joins Monroe and Rosalee in a search for a cure for Nick

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the murder of memoir writer Sweetie Ness. Clues initially lead them to suspect his stalker, agent and a groupie, but after digging deeper into the victim's past, they begin to consider the possibility he was not who he claimed to be

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A woman's murder leads Lupo and Bernard to learn she was secretly in conflict with her husband, going against his desire that their family lead an environmentally friendly life. The case is complicated further when a dead passenger and drugs turn up in the car she was using, with the complexity continuing in the courtroom as Cutter suspects someone has been tampering with evidence. Guest starring Ned Beatty (Deliverance) and Sherry Stringfield (ER)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The shooting of a board-member at an exclusive private school, and a man's ambitions for his offspring are stretched to the limit when he admits to the crime, despite the fact his son was the gunman. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The team investigates when a journalist is found murdered, but the case presents new evidence for an old crime involving a man serving a life sentence for spying for the Chinese government. Keith Carradine guest stars

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the brutal murder of a TV reporter who had been alleging that a billionaire Wall Street investment adviser was a fraud. Evidence of love triangles and hedge fund fraud soon comes to the fore. Crime drama, starring Jeremy Sisto and Anthony Anderson

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Logan and Briscoe investigate the murder of a psychiatrist, and their inquiries lead them to suspect the killer is either the victim's former husband, her obstructive father or a patient with multiple personality disorder

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and his team investigate the bizarre deaths of a marine and two of his friends at a stag party. Elsewhere, Gibbs tries to persuade Vance to allow Ziva back into the NCIS. However, she must first pass a barrage of psychological evaluations. Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A blogger who accused the NCIS of a cover-up is found dead, and Gibbs and his team are forced to reopen a closed case to find the killer. However, state police suspect the special agents may have been involved in the murder and take control of the investigation. Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a marine off the coast of Tanzania - a case complicated by the discovery that he had a link to Ziva. The mystery deepens even further with the recovery of wreckage from a sunken freighter. Starring Mark Harmon, Cote de Pablo, David McCallum, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A rapist's claims that he is genetically predisposed to commit sex crimes creates outrage among the team

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: When young investment banker Zoe White accuses billionaire client Eli Colton of rape, a past 'victim' of Eli's is discovered, though her payoff included a nondisclosure agreement. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A computer genius is accused of murdering his girlfriend, but responds to the team's investigation by threatening to expose their darkest secrets

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An aspiring Olympic athlete claims she is a rape victim, but the publicity could threaten her Olympic future, and reveal some shocking details about her sordid personal life

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson discovers that a young man facing rape charges is the son of a sergeant - her first police partner - who comes to her asking to cash in on an a favour that she owes him. Mariska Hargitay stars

programme.title: Grimm

Juliette learns she is the key to restoring Nick's powers, while Trubel is warned of a threat to her mentor's life

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Nick and Juliette undergo a ritual to restore his powers, but before he can be sure it has worked he is plunged into a case involving human sacrifice as a group of lizards uses a stretch of highway for a horrifying Wesen ritual

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms items reclaimed from a recycling centre in Walsall, West Midlands, featuring a pair of chairs, a plant stand and printed bean sacks

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Hyacinth Miller from London first visited Barbados 14 years ago, and now that she's retired, wants to permanently relocate there and spend time in the garden. With her son Leon along to help choose the right property, Jasmine Harman shows Hyacinth five properties in the south of the island, but do any of them have her packing her bags for a new life in the sun?

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms items reclaimed from a recycling centre in Walsall, West Midlands, featuring a pair of chairs, a plant stand and printed bean sacks

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Philip Serrell visit the antiques shops of Yorkshire, along the way meeting a trombone-maker and discovering the history of the rocking horse

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Philip Serrell scour the antiques shops around Sheffield to find something that will give them the edge at the auction. Natasha goes out of her comfort zone when considering some 18th-century pottery, but feels more at home when she spots something from the 1970s. Philip opts for some typically 'Serrell' items, as well as chewing over more prehistoric stuff. He also goes out of his way to prove that chivalry isn't dead. On the road, Philip visits a factory using the world-famous Sheffield steel to make some very fine tools, and Natasha heads to the woods for a spot of foraging and some free food. It's going to be a nail biter at their final auction at Lytham St Annes, Lancashire

programme.title: My Feet Are Killing Me
programme.desc: Dr Vincent performs a delicate surgery when she treats a toddler born with 12 toes. Plus, things get tense when a patient wakes up in the middle of their operation

programme.title: Embarrassing Bodies
programme.desc: Pixie McKenna investigates new surgery techniques involving the anus, and helps a man suffering with hot testicles. Dawn Harper meets a woman who had a stroke at just 45, revealing whether electrotherapy can help her regain more of her bodily functions, and investigates the worrying trend of buying legal highs online. Christian Jessen is joined by Nurse Joy Tickle to help treat assorted skin conditions for visitors in Birmingham, and in Magaluf there's a look at the serious side effects of spiking someone's drink

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: The Restorers
